ideas.title: Contact Sheet
ideas.hook: Every phone gets a different secret prompt, you each have ninety seconds to photograph something in the room that answers yours, and the group walks away with one anonymous contact sheet nobody can fully decode.

ideas.title: Skein
ideas.hook: Everyone owns one thread of a single friendship bracelet, committing each knot blind at the same instant — your mismatches don't lose, they weave themselves into the pattern you all take home.

ideas.title: Crosstalk
ideas.hook: It's a live panel show where every guest must land their secret line, but the moment two of you speak at once the broadcast dissolves into unwatchable crosstalk and you both lose your turn.

ideas.title: Overbooked
ideas.hook: A plane fills up seat by seat, everyone picks silently and at once, and the instant two of you grab the same seat you're both bumped off the flight.

ideas.title: Wildcat
ideas.hook: A sealed-bid oil auction where each phone shows you a different survey of the same tract — and winning almost always means you were the fool who overpaid.

ideas.title: Going Once
ideas.hook: A Dutch auction of pure nerve: the price falls, your finger hovers over BUY, and only your phone knows what the prize is actually worth to you.

ideas.title: Boneyard
ideas.hook: changedetection.io alerts you when a webpage changes; Boneyard alerts you when your own saved links quietly die — and buries each one under a tombstone showing its last living snapshot.

ideas.title: Preflight
ideas.hook: A menubar wiretap that shows, in real time, exactly how many tokens your AI coding CLI burns *before* it ever reads your prompt — broken down by system prompt, tool schemas, and history.

ideas.title: Lyrebird
ideas.hook: Compose a synth patch that fools a real bird-ID neural net into hearing an endangered warbler — an adversarial-audio puzzle game hiding inside a citizen-science tool.

ideas.title: Alibi
ideas.hook: The group reconstructs a suspect's night from private clue cards, but one player's phone quietly corrupted a clue — and the contradiction it creates is how you catch them.

ideas.title: Crossed Wire
ideas.hook: Everyone privately plays the same voicemail, but the imposter's copy swapped one detail — and they'll swear their version is the real one.

ideas.title: Undercut
ideas.hook: Secretly price your lemonade stand against your friends' — the crowd all buys from the single cheapest stand, but if two of you tie for lowest, the customers riot and you both get nothing.

ideas.title: Crossed Wires
ideas.hook: Route your secret wire across a shared circuit board without seeing anyone else's — everyone greedily hugs the short center path, and any two traces that touch the same cell short out and die.

ideas.title: Carry
ideas.hook: Everyone's phone hides a fistful of numbers, the host demands 'exactly 12, using exactly three of you,' and the room has to shout arithmetic across the table and then get precisely the right three people to slam COMMIT in the same heartbeat.

ideas.title: Antiphon
ideas.hook: Every phone holds loose halves of spoken commands, and the only way to complete one is to broadcast your fragment until a stranger across the room realizes they're holding your other half — then say it back-to-back before the mic clogs.

ideas.title: Typosquat
ideas.hook: A defensive-security puzzle game where you play the attacker registering lookalike domains in a sandbox, and the real eth-phishing-detect blocklist is the boss trying to flag you — you win by learning exactly which tricks the detector catches.

ideas.title: Seedbank
ideas.hook: A conservation tycoon over your real qBittorrent stats where dying, near-dead torrents are endangered species — the last seeder of a rare 2009 Linux ISO earns you more than a thousand-peer blockbuster.

ideas.title: Boilerplate
ideas.hook: A roguelike where the dungeon is your own agent's bloated system prompt — every floor accretes tool defs, few-shot examples, and safety preamble, and your token budget is the HP bleaking out before you ever reach the real request.

ideas.title: Watermark
ideas.hook: Everyone answers the same prompt, but each phone is secretly handed one mandatory word it must smuggle into the answer — and you win the round only if nobody can pick your writing out of the lineup.

ideas.title: Relief
ideas.hook: Four phones each secretly carve one hidden quadrant of the same woodblock, and nobody sees the whole image until the host screen pulls a single print you'll want to keep.

ideas.title: House Rules
ideas.hook: You're all scoring the exact same hand of cards — but one player's private rulebook has a single rule quietly swapped, and their honest total gives them away.

ideas.title: Parallax
ideas.hook: Everyone photographs the same tabletop of blocks from their own private seat — but one witness is describing an angle that geometry says cannot exist.

ideas.title: Coast
ideas.hook: Your phone silently scores every drive on smoothness — hard brakes and jerky acceleration cost you — and ranks you against every other commuter on your exact road, so the dead time behind the wheel becomes a ladder you climb by doing nothing but driving well.

ideas.title: Dog-Ear
ideas.hook: A reading league where you and friends read the same article, then get ambushed days later with an LLM quiz — points go to whoever actually *retained* it, and every point decays over time.

ideas.title: Rime
ideas.hook: An always-on daemon quietly grows a field of frost crystals seeded by your shell history — the commands you repeat most accrete the thickest, densest ferns, until a year later your wallpaper is a portrait of your habits made of ice.

ideas.title: Tuning Fork
ideas.hook: Each player wears earbuds and slides a private tone only they can hear, and the room must silently drag every pitch into one unison note using nothing but a shared dissonance meter.

ideas.title: Flash Mob
ideas.hook: Everyone taps a private flash on their own phone, and the room must silently phase-lock into one crisp strobe on the TV like a swarm of fireflies learning to blink together.

ideas.title: Sight Unseen
ideas.hook: A storage-unit blind auction where every phone secretly knows one true thing about one padlocked locker — so you're bidding real money on rumors only you can hear.

ideas.title: Palate
ideas.hook: A wine-cellar draft where every phone hides a different secret scoring rubric, so the same bottle is a steal for you and garbage for the player reaching for it too.

ideas.title: Sway
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly gets a different word to smuggle into the model's mind, then you all blind-write priming sentences into one shared context and fight a hidden tug-of-war over what the model predicts next.

ideas.title: Governor
ideas.hook: A live typing tightrope where your phone's private perplexity meter punishes every interesting word, so you race to build the most gloriously boring sentence before the ceiling clamps shut.

ideas.title: Ostracon
ideas.hook: Play the vizier's scribe rationing grain, fish, and beer to the men digging Pharaoh's tomb — miss too many deliveries and they stage history's first recorded strike, sitting down at the mortuary temple until you pay up.

ideas.title: Hockey Stick
ideas.hook: Fantasy sports for open source — draft a roster off GitHub Trending, then sweat the weekly waiver wire as your picks either moon on star-velocity or flatline into deadweight.

ideas.title: Ulp
ideas.hook: A wallpaper painted from your machine's own floating-point soul — the tiny rounding errors your CPU makes computing tanh and exp — that quietly mutates like tree rings every time an OS or microcode update shifts the bits.

ideas.title: Cast
ideas.hook: Each player blindly choreographs one limb of a shared creature by waving their phone in the air, and the composited monster spins on the TV as an exportable keepsake nobody could have designed alone.

ideas.title: Overspray
ideas.hook: Everyone spray-paints one shared wall through a stencil only they can see, each secretly steering the mural toward a different picture — the glorious mess is the keepsake.

ideas.title: Dead Ringer
ideas.hook: Everyone silently builds a little avatar from the same parts bin, and the room only wins if all your strangers turn out to be the exact same person.

ideas.title: Dovetail
ideas.hook: Each of you draws one blind panel of a single shared picture, and the only way to win is to make your strokes meet a neighbor's you were never allowed to see.

ideas.title: Potluck
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly brings one dish to a shared table, but two people bringing the same thing means both dishes hit the floor.

ideas.title: Split Second
ideas.hook: A clock hand sweeps once; everyone secretly stabs a moment in time, but tap too close to someone else and you both flatline.

ideas.title: Autopay
ideas.hook: A Luck-Be-a-Landlord-style slot roguelike where the symbols on your reels are your actual recurring subscriptions, and you have to make rent every month by discovering which ones synergize and which are dead weight.

ideas.title: Firehose
ideas.hook: A Vampire-Survivors-style bullet-heaven where lines from your real logs swarm you as enemies and the regex rules you write are auto-firing weapons that vaporize matching patterns.

ideas.title: Preamble
ideas.hook: Competitive prompt-golf: two players get the same failing coding task and race to make a fixed model pass it using the fewest tokens of context they dare to send.

ideas.title: Tumblers
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly spins the dials of their own combination lock, and the vault opens only when all your locks read the exact same number — with nothing but a 'warmer/colder' agreement meter to guide you.

ideas.title: Planchette
ideas.hook: Every phone is a finger on the same Ouija planchette, and the room must silently pull it toward letters to spell a word nobody agreed on.

ideas.title: Warmer
ideas.hook: One blindfolded player roams the room humming a tone from their phone while everyone else must silently drift to their own secret orbit distance — some hugging close, some pinned to the far wall — as the beacon keeps moving.

ideas.title: Camber
ideas.hook: Stand around the TV like edges of a tilting table and lean your phones together to roll one shared ball into its hole — but which way your tilt pushes depends on which wall you're standing at.

ideas.title: Odds-On
ideas.hook: One friend is handed a secret 30-second stunt; everyone else becomes the bookie, privately laying money on whether they'll choke — while the performer never sees the line.

ideas.title: Big Ticket
ideas.hook: Everyone reads the same menu, but each phone secretly stacks chips on which dish is priciest — and only you can see the one insider price you were slipped.

ideas.title: Timeout
ideas.hook: fail2ban, but it bans *you*: a daemon that catches you thrashing the same failing command and locks you into a 20-second breathing timer before the next retry.

ideas.title: Chaff
ideas.hook: Inverts Anubis: instead of gating AI scrapers out with proof-of-work, it detects them and feeds them an infinite maze of believable, watermarked, fabricated pages — burning their crawl budget and tracing who's harvesting you.

ideas.title: State Line
ideas.hook: Turns a trucker's GPS breadcrumb trail into an audit-proof IFTA fuel-tax report by splitting mileage at the exact state line and reconciling fuel receipts.

ideas.title: Understudy
ideas.hook: Every player performs a short scene from their own private 'actor's sides' — and one script has a single doctored line that quietly contradicts the story, so the imposter breaks continuity without knowing they're the liar.

ideas.title: Backbeat
ideas.hook: Everyone claps to a private metronome and one secretly runs 9% fast — so the imposter genuinely feels in time while they rush the whole room out of the pocket.

ideas.title: Marionette
ideas.hook: You control exactly one leg of a floppy shared ragdoll you cannot see, while the only player watching the whole stage screams timing at you to flop the body across a pit.

ideas.title: Lighthouse
ideas.hook: You're piloting a boat home through unseen rocks in the pitch dark, and the only person who can see the whole harbor rations a single sweeping beam of light across all of you at once.

ideas.title: Cliffhanger
ideas.hook: The whole room watches one suspense clip while a live payout multiplier climbs — cash out on your own phone before the moment lands, but you can't see when anyone else got cold feet.

ideas.title: Rundown
ideas.hook: The group turns their own takeout menu into a Top-6 countdown show, then secretly bets on a ranking they all helped write but nobody has seen.

ideas.title: Clean Claim
ideas.hook: Half of a dental office's denials are dumb — a missing X-ray, the wrong tooth-number format, a narrative Delta wants but Cigna doesn't — so Clean Claim pre-flights each claim against that specific payer's attachment rules before it's ever submitted.

ideas.title: Leak Rate
ideas.hook: Every time a tech tops off a big rooftop AC unit, the EPA wants it logged and the leak rate calculated — Leak Rate turns that dreaded paper binder into a phone tap that also warns you before a system trips the mandatory-repair threshold.

ideas.title: Cohort
ideas.hook: The new federal rule ties financial aid to whether grads out-earn a high-schooler — Cohort tells a small college if each program is about to flunk that test, before the Department of Education does.

ideas.title: Spread
ideas.hook: The model reveals eight candidate next-words for a blank — with the odds hidden — and you privately stake your claim on the one you think it secretly favors, but anyone who claimed the same word splits the pot.

ideas.title: Contraband
ideas.hook: You're handed one weird word you must smuggle into a sentence so smoothly the language model never flinches — while everyone else hunts your sentence for the one spike that gives you away.

ideas.title: Mirror Image
ideas.hook: Everyone gives spatial clues about the same little diagram on their phone — but one player's diagram is flipped left-for-right, and they must silently invert every clue in their head or get caught saying the tree is on the wrong side.

ideas.title: Voiceover
ideas.hook: Everyone privately hears the same 40-second narrated memory through their own phone — except one player's dub quietly swapped a few facts, and nobody, not even that player, is told whose reality is the wrong one.

ideas.title: Order Up
ideas.hook: The TV shows a restaurant menu; before anyone reveals what they'd actually order, you privately bet your chips on exactly what each friend at the table will pick.

ideas.title: Called It
ideas.hook: The host TV slowly un-blurs a single photo while everyone privately races to lock their guess — for a payout that shrinks the clearer the picture gets.

ideas.title: Vacancy
ideas.hook: A daily deduction puzzle where you scrub a week of a smart home's sensor logs to find the one day an intruder was quietly living someone else's routine.

ideas.title: Fair Warning
ideas.hook: A release-gate bot that reads your actual public API surface and calls you a liar when you tag a breaking change as a patch bump.

ideas.title: Chirp
ideas.hook: The dashboard that knows every smoke detector, door-lock, and thermostat battery in your apartment portfolio is about to die — before the tenant calls at 3 a.m.

ideas.title: Lion's Share
ideas.hook: One player secretly carves a treasure hoard into bundles on their phone; everyone else secretly claims a bundle at the same instant — and the divider only wins if their portions were fair enough that no one fought over the good one.

ideas.title: Fire Sale
ideas.hook: A descending-price Dutch auction where the clock ticks the price down in real time and your thumb is the only thing between you and a steal — but only your phone knows your budget and which lots are secretly worth double to you.

ideas.title: Doomscroll
ideas.hook: A deliberately boring clip plays on the TV while your phone floods you with an irresistible meme feed — secretly bet on which friend caves and starts scrolling first, as your own phone snitches on your willpower.

ideas.title: Deadpan
ideas.hook: Watch a cringe compilation while secretly betting on which friends will crack up first — and your phone privately dares you to fake a reaction and bust everyone's bets.

ideas.title: Earshot
ideas.hook: Everyone holds their phone to their ear and hears the same voicemail at the same instant — but one person's clip has a single word swapped, and reconstructing the message out loud is how you catch them.

ideas.title: Tangent
ideas.hook: Everyone answers the same rapid-fire prompts on their own phone — except one player has been reading a slightly different question the whole time and doesn't fully know it.

ideas.title: Daisy Chain
ideas.hook: A forensic-accounting deduction game where you play the short-seller unwinding a web of vendor deals to expose the circular revenue loop before the company IPOs.

ideas.title: Vernacular
ideas.hook: A party drawing game that rewards the weirdo: draw the prompt in the way *least* like everyone else — while still barely recognizable to the machine.

ideas.title: Water Glass
ideas.hook: A physics puzzle where liquid walls shatter like brittle glass if you strike them fast enough — but flow, seal, and drown you if you push too slow.

ideas.title: Crop
ideas.hook: One photo fills the TV and everyone secretly pinch-zooms their own phone to frame the detail they think everyone else will frame — the room wins when all your hidden crops land on the same thing.

ideas.title: Asterism
ideas.hook: Nine stars sit on the TV and every phone silently connects its own dots — the room wins only when everyone, with no talking, has drawn the exact same constellation.

ideas.title: Even Split
ideas.hook: One player must carve a pile of loot into fair bundles knowing only their own secret prices — then watch the reveal expose exactly how badly everyone got robbed.

ideas.title: Dutch Angle
ideas.hook: A price clock ticks downward on the TV while everyone's thumb hovers over GRAB — buy the haunted lava lamp now for too much, or wait a beat and pray nobody steals it out from under you.

ideas.title: Baby Monitor
ideas.hook: Keep the baby asleep by staying dead silent — except one of you is secretly the Gremlin, tapping private 'tickles' to random phones to make people blurt, and every phone's own mic snitches on its owner.

ideas.title: Powder Keg
ideas.hook: A sound-triggered bomb: each phone privately holds one digit and one ordering clue, and you must enter the code in the right order — but the detonator listens, so a single frustrated groan blows the room up.

ideas.title: Prototype
ideas.hook: Draw a 'cup', a 'house', a 'bird' — then watch how far your mental default sits from the rest of the world's, using 50 million real doodles as the mirror.

ideas.title: Malpractice
ideas.hook: An adversarial code-golf game: given a clean React component, sneak in the nastiest anti-pattern you can that still slips past the AI 'doctor' linter — you win by being subtly wrong, not loudly broken.

ideas.title: Round Trip
ideas.hook: A tycoon-puzzle where you play the invisible hand of the GPU boom, wiring cash in circles between cloud startups so everyone's valuation balloons — until an auditor pulls one thread and the whole ring implodes.

ideas.title: Longhand
ideas.hook: The whole room secretly finger-writes the same phrase at the same time, the host weaves the handwriting into one anonymous poster, and you win by being the person no one can pick out of the lineup.

ideas.title: Music Box
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly owns one instrument lane of a blind step-sequencer and layers a beat by ear alone, until the room has jammed a loop nobody could have written solo — exported as a keepsake track.

ideas.title: Detune
ideas.hook: Three people hum into their phones to build one fat chord — but any two voices that drift within a semitone of each other collapse into ugly beating dissonance.

ideas.title: Swerve
ideas.hook: Everyone races to type a unique answer while the TV leaks each player's first letter live — and any two answers that collide both explode.

ideas.title: Family Style
ideas.hook: Before anyone says what they're getting, secretly bet on what everyone at the table will actually order off the same menu — points for reading your friends, and points for ordering something nobody saw coming.

ideas.title: Steam
ideas.hook: Everyone watches the same 60-second clip, but you bet on how it ends inside a private parimutuel pool — and one of you was secretly tipped off, so learn to read whose money is moving the line.

ideas.title: Cold Read
ideas.hook: A deadpan CLI fortune-teller that reads your lockfile like tarot and predicts, with unsettling specificity, which dependency will betray you next.

ideas.title: Comment Period
ideas.hook: One notification feed for the five clunky city permit portals a contractor otherwise has to log into every morning to see if plan review left a comment.

ideas.title: Quick, Where?
ideas.hook: Geoguessr for doodles — you're shown a stranger's scribble of a light switch or a taxi and you guess which country's hand drew it.

ideas.title: Canary
ideas.hook: The one player who can see the mine map isn't allowed to speak — they can only blink a single arrow onto each blind miner's lamp before the gas gets them.

ideas.title: Fathom
ideas.hook: One player reads the only chart of a rock-strewn harbor and must talk two blindfolded captains through the fog before the tide runs out.

ideas.title: Brim
ideas.hook: Hold your phone dead-level like a brimful glass and walk it across the room to a private compass bearing — tilt too far and you spill, and everyone has to arrive without a drop.

ideas.title: Storey
ideas.hook: Your phone is a private altimeter — crouch, stand, or climb onto a chair to plant your floor of a skyscraper that only rises when everyone holds a different height at once.

ideas.title: Mondegreen
ideas.hook: The whole room belts a karaoke song in unison off their phones — but the imposter's lyrics were quietly rewritten on one line, and at the chorus their wrong words ring out over everyone else's.

ideas.title: Retcon
ideas.hook: Everyone privately reads the same wordless comic and writes its moral — but one player's copy has a single panel swapped, so their moral is quietly, damningly wrong.

ideas.title: Dazzle
ideas.hook: A living-room stealth game where your phone is a killer drone running real person-detection, and everyone has to cross the room without being seen — break your silhouette or get spotted.

ideas.title: Load-Bearing
ideas.hook: The mischievous inverse of 'rebuild Redis from scratch': a roguelike that hands you a demolition charge and dares you to delete as much of your own repo as you can before the test suite dies.

ideas.title: Dead Reckoning
ideas.hook: A solo-unsupported ocean-crossing survival sim where your real Garmin metrics are the boat: every logged mile makes way, bad sleep drifts you backward, and low HRV sinks morale until you're becalmed.

ideas.title: Plaque
ideas.hook: The room secretly co-writes a pompous award certificate for one unsuspecting friend — each phone drafts one blind clause while the honoree's screen quietly lies to them — and prints it as a keepsake they take home.

ideas.title: Foley
ideas.hook: Each phone is secretly handed one sound to perform with your mouth and hands, everyone records at once in the blind, and the server layers your chaos into a 15-second radio-drama scene the room keeps.

ideas.title: Mouthpiece
ideas.hook: The one player allowed to speak is the only one who can't see the puzzle, while everyone who holds a clue is gagged by their own phone's mic — so the knowers must silently steer a blindfolded mouth.

ideas.title: Bated Breath
ideas.hook: A silent standoff where most players just have to not make a sound — except the one or two secretly ordered to break the silence first, and everyone's own phone is the snitch that catches only their mouth.

ideas.title: Table Stakes
ideas.hook: The moment your group stares at a takeout menu becomes a hidden betting game: secretly lock your own order, then privately wager on who caves on the salad and which dish the table piles onto.

ideas.title: Bandwagon
ideas.hook: While a competition show plays on the TV, everyone runs a secret stock portfolio on the contestants — so you can cheer the beloved grandma out loud while quietly short-selling her.

ideas.title: Squatter
ideas.hook: An invented tenant quietly lives a believable, weather-aware daily life through your smart home while you're on vacation — so the lights lie convincingly to burglars.

ideas.title: Well Fed
ideas.hook: Valheim's food system for your actual diet: three meal slots, buffs that decay, and eating the same sad sandwich twice is a wasted slot.

ideas.title: Photobomb
ideas.hook: Tell your telescope exactly which Starlink streaks will slash through your two-hour exposure — and when to click the shutter to dodge them.

ideas.title: Kickback
ideas.hook: The group has $100 and a shared shopping list — but the imposter's phone is quietly quoting them the wrong prices, so every total they blurt out is off by just enough.

ideas.title: Overdub
ideas.hook: Everyone listens privately to the same 20-second voicemail — except the imposter's copy swapped one word, and now they have to recount a story they never quite heard.

ideas.title: Middle Ground
ideas.hook: Each phone is secretly pulled toward a different word, and the whole room must still silently meet on one shared word by abandoning its own bias.

ideas.title: Muck
ideas.hook: Everyone holds a secret hand of symbols and must silently cull down to the exact same last card — without ever seeing another player's hand.

ideas.title: Deep Cut
ideas.hook: A shuffle playlist becomes an over/under market where you privately bet on how many friends will recognize each song before the snippet even plays.

ideas.title: House Special
ideas.hook: The takeout menu becomes a secret prediction market where you can rig the vote by changing your own order to pump your bets.

ideas.title: Streak
ideas.hook: Before you open a 5-minute exposure, Streak tells astrophotographers exactly when a Starlink will slice through their framed field of view — turning free public TLEs into a per-shutter go/no-go call.

ideas.title: Reactor Four
ideas.hook: Your homelab, rendered as a Soviet nuclear control room — CPU load spins damped analog needles, disk fills nixie counters, and killing a service triggers a Cyrillic SCRAM klaxon.

ideas.title: Dog Whistle
ideas.hook: Craft an image where a human reads 'CANCEL SUBSCRIPTION' but the vision model swears it says 'CONFIRM PAYMENT' — the mischievous inverse of Ghost Font, scored as a red-team party game.

ideas.title: Craving
ideas.hook: Everyone drafts from one shared pantry on the TV, but each phone hides a different secret craving — so the real game is guessing what your rivals want and snatching it before they can.

ideas.title: Markdown
ideas.hook: A single absurd lot drops in price on the TV while every phone secretly knows only its own max — first thumb to slap BUY steals it, and nobody can read your nerve.

ideas.title: Unsigned
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly writes an anonymous kind note to a randomly assigned player, and the game you win is leaving with a card full of compliments while nobody can prove which one you wrote.

ideas.title: Zoetrope
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly draws one frame of the same short animation while glimpsing only a ghost of the frame before theirs, and the host stitches the wobbling result into a looping GIF you all keep.

ideas.title: Clave
ideas.hook: The TV is a slow metronome and an empty drum machine; each phone secretly owns one clap-slot and privately whispers 'early / late' from its own mic, so the room becomes a human beat you can only lock by spreading out.

ideas.title: Trim
ideas.hook: The host TV is one tilting table and a rolling ball — but each player's phone only controls one direction, so you steer the maze with your bodies scattered around the room.

ideas.title: Hand-Wave
ideas.hook: A daily deduction puzzle that hands you a slick AI-written proof with exactly one lie buried in it, and dares you to find the step where the machine bluffed.

ideas.title: Zeitgeber
ideas.hook: A screensaver clock that quietly redraws its own dial from your body's real rhythm, until by New Year's the twelve hours have stretched and shrunk into the shape of your year.

ideas.title: Off-Ramp
ideas.hook: A compliance robot that drives your own signup-to-cancel funnel every night and hands you a dated badge proving no dark patterns before an AG does it for you.

ideas.title: Head Table
ideas.hook: One player secretly holds the correct seating chart; the rest are blind guests who each carry one petty grudge they can't reveal, and the host must nudge everyone into the right order before someone storms off.

ideas.title: Overcast
ideas.hook: You're an air-traffic controller with the only radar; your two friends are pilots socked in the clouds who can't see the runway, each other, or anything but their own trembling instrument panel — talk them down before the fuel runs out.

ideas.title: Dibs
ideas.hook: A grid of treasure everyone grabs at once in secret — but any tile two people reach for shatters, and each of you sees a different secret price tag.

ideas.title: Blind Corner
ideas.hook: Everyone tilt-steers their own invisible dot through the same maze, and the only thing that can kill you is a teammate you can't see.

ideas.title: Fingerprint
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly writes one sentence, a tiny model turns each into a jagged 'fever chart' of where it got surprised, and the room matches the anonymized, word-less curves back to their authors.

ideas.title: Iron Out
ideas.hook: A garbled sentence sits on the TV; each phone secretly owns a few of its words and must smooth them blind — coordinating only through the one shared perplexity thermometer as it falls toward the target line.

ideas.title: Overhead
ideas.hook: A self-hosted poster that quietly logs every satellite crossing your patch of zenith each night, growing a year-long barcode of low-earth-orbit getting more crowded.

ideas.title: Headcount
ideas.hook: Your dependency tree, run all year as a satirical company that hires, promotes, lays off, and suffers the occasional trade-secret defection — with a straight-faced annual report waiting on December 31.

ideas.title: Test Pit
ideas.hook: Excavate your own hard drive like an archaeological dig — trowel down through mtime strata and name the vanished 'cultures' who left ten thousand identical node_modules potsherds.

ideas.title: Talk Is Cheap
ideas.hook: Every player gets a tiny secret budget of words for the whole round, the team dies if prompts go unanswered — so it's a silent game of chicken over who spends their breath.

ideas.title: Pin Drop
ideas.hook: A silent heist where each phone whispers a different private clue into your ear — but only while the whole room stays quiet enough to hear a pin drop.

ideas.title: Sitter
ideas.hook: Everyone draws one blind region of the same face — each with a secret emotional brief — and the reveal stitches a gorgeously incoherent group portrait you keep.

ideas.title: Blackout
ideas.hook: Everyone redacts their own words on one shared page to carve a group blackout poem — while secretly keeping one word alive without getting caught protecting it.

ideas.title: Prop Bet
ideas.hook: A live prop-betting layer for whatever show you're already watching — each phone secretly holds a different hand of 'will happen' cards and you wager, hidden, on the same scene.

ideas.title: Dark Pool
ideas.hook: The takeout menu becomes a hidden order book — secretly buy shares in the dish you think the table will really order while lobbying loudly against it.

ideas.title: Power Nap
ideas.hook: An idle game that runs backwards: your homelab's real containers are sleepy creatures, and you get richer the longer you keep them powered *down*—every watt of scale-to-zero idle time is currency you spend to make them nap harder.

ideas.title: Crate
ideas.hook: A roguelike deckbuilder where the cards are your own MP3s—draft a setlist from your real music library and survive the night, but clashing keys and tempo cliffs drain the crowd's energy until they walk out.

ideas.title: Reorg
ideas.hook: Your package-lock.json is a bloated corporation and you're the ruthless new CEO—lay off middle-manager dependencies, watch the cascade of firings ripple down the org chart, and bank the bytes you saved.

ideas.title: Blindside
ideas.hook: A stealth game where one runner threads a grid full of guard vision-cones that each sentry can see only their own of — so the guards have to describe their coverage out loud while the runner listens for the gap.

ideas.title: Seam
ideas.hook: One giant match-3 board sliced into private strips, where the only big-scoring clears are the ones that cross the seam and demand two players tap in the same heartbeat.

ideas.title: Quiet Car
ideas.hook: Musical chairs where the chairs are quiet spots in the room and each player secretly needs a different amount of silence — so you fight over the good corner without knowing who actually needs it.

ideas.title: Cardinal
ideas.hook: You and a secret partner must silently point your phones at the same wall — while the other hidden pair claims a different one, and everyone's watching your body give it away.

ideas.title: Off-Color
ideas.hook: Everyone taps the tile that matches the named color — but the imposter's phone quietly shifted one swatch, so they keep reaching for the wrong shade.

ideas.title: False Friend
ideas.hook: The room translates one gibberish sentence together, but the imposter's private phrasebook has a single word that lies.

ideas.title: Knock
ideas.hook: Hold your phone near a running engine and it FFTs the growl against a firing-order model to tell you the cylinder that's misfiring — a combustion toy turned pocket mechanic.

ideas.title: Blame War
ideas.hook: Turns `git log` into an interactive war atlas where files are contested territory, merge conflicts and reverts are battles, and commit messages are the casus belli.

ideas.title: Tin Road
ideas.hook: A dependency-fragility auditor disguised as a Late Bronze Age trade-collapse map — pull one keystone package and watch which of your apps go dark like cities cut off from tin.

ideas.title: Crosshair
ideas.hook: Every phone secretly holds one cell of a shared word-grid, everyone gives a one-word clue for their own hidden target at once, and the silent room must place all the anonymous clues onto the right squares before the timer burns down.

ideas.title: Second Guess
ideas.hook: Everyone writes a secret clue for the same word at once — but any two clues that match cancel out, so your real opponent is the obvious idea forming in five heads you can't see.

ideas.title: Terror Radius
ideas.hook: Three phones frantically tap private skill-checks to fix generators while one player hunts them across the TV map — and every missed tap flashes only YOUR location to the killer.

ideas.title: Hard Drop
ideas.hook: One Tetris well on the TV, but every player privately steers their own falling piece into their own band of the stack — and a line only clears when all of you fill the row together.

ideas.title: On Three
ideas.hook: Each phone holds one dial of a shared vault whose setting depends on a number another player has to shout — set your dial from what you hear, then everyone release on the same beat or the lock resets.

ideas.title: Cold Read
ideas.hook: A breaking-news bulletin is scattered in fragments across your phones, only one mic can be live at a time, and nobody knows the running order until you read each other's lines aloud.

ideas.title: Overweight
ideas.hook: A daily higher-or-lower duel where two famous websites square off and you guess which one ships more bloat — then watch the real megabytes drop like a game-show reveal.

ideas.title: Livestock
ideas.hook: A creature-collector that reads your bank feed and turns every recurring subscription into a Palworld-style monster you have to feed cash each month — so you fatten the good ones, breed bundles, and cull the freeloaders on a lowest-feed-cost leaderboard.

ideas.title: In the Wild
ideas.hook: A forecasting market where you stake points on which freshly-published CVE proof-of-concept gets weaponized — added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog — before its clock runs out.

ideas.title: Offbeat
ideas.hook: Each phone is one drum voice in a shared groove, and any two taps that land at the same instant erupt into an ugly clash — so the room must silently interleave its hits into a clean polyrhythm where nobody ever coincides.

ideas.title: Jinx
ideas.hook: Everyone answers the same open prompt at once, but any answer two people land on gets both jinxed to zero — and a live 'heat' gauge lets you feel the collision coming while you both refuse to blink first.

ideas.title: Countersign
ideas.hook: The host lights a link between two random players and one reads their secret challenge aloud — the other must find the matching countersign buried in decoys on their own phone and shout it back before the sentry line goes cold.

ideas.title: Safe Hands
ideas.hook: You each control one wheel of a shared vault lock — but the only clues you hold are about everyone else's wheel, so you must talk the whole combination into existence and slam it home together.

ideas.title: Downstream
ideas.hook: A booster draft where everyone opens a pack at once and your phone secretly remembers every card you've passed — so when a pack wheels back to you gutted, you alone know who upstream is starving your suit.

ideas.title: Wildcat
ideas.hook: Everyone's drilling the same oil field, but your phone shows only YOUR secret geologist's estimate — bid the lease high on a bad hunch and you'll own a dry hole.

ideas.title: Use After Free
ideas.hook: A Phasmophobia-style co-op horror where the ghost haunting your codebase is a memory-safety bug, and your team gathers undefined-behavior 'evidence' to identify it before it corrupts the build.

ideas.title: Switching Cost
ideas.hook: A marketplace where product teams pay vetted power-users of a rival tool to switch to it exclusively for a month and file a structured defection diary of every friction point.

ideas.title: Quiet NaN
ideas.hook: A taint-tracer for dataframes that pins the exact row, column, and operation where a NaN or null first crept in — then shows every downstream cell it silently poisoned.

ideas.title: Unidentified
ideas.hook: A roguelike shelf of mystery potions and a boss with a secret weakness — everyone quaffs a different unknown vial at the same instant, privately learns what it did to *them*, and the party has to build the color→effect table by science before the boss timer runs out.

ideas.title: Sequence Break
ideas.hook: One tiny metroidvania map on the TV, one shared runner token, and the only way past each colored gate is the phone secretly holding that key — some doors need two players to turn theirs at the exact same instant.

ideas.title: Tote
ideas.hook: The TV shows a menu the group is about to pick from; secretly bet your chips on what the room will crown — nail the underbet dark horse and clean up, back the obvious favorite and win pennies.

ideas.title: Tout
ideas.hook: Everyone watches the same dumb video clip, but your phone secretly slips you one real insider tip about what's coming — cash in without tipping the sharks that you know.

ideas.title: Blackball
ideas.hook: Eight options sit on the table and each phone secretly, silently drops black balls on the ones it wants gone — the room wins by converging to exactly ONE survivor, and loses the instant it nervously nukes them all.

ideas.title: Same Page
ideas.hook: Every phone is a private window into one shared cluttered image, and the room must silently pan and zoom until you're all framing the exact same tiny detail — without ever seeing where anyone else is looking.

ideas.title: Busy Signal
ideas.hook: A $35 Raspberry Pi that listens to the ambient radio hum of a room and publishes a live 'how packed is it right now' bar for gyms and cafes—no cameras, no thousand-dollar counter.

ideas.title: Hood
ideas.hook: The pocket tool that turns a hood-cleaner's before/after photos into an NFPA 96-compliant dated certificate and never lets them forget the next-due rebooking.

ideas.title: Unshipped
ideas.hook: Watches your competitor's JavaScript bundles every night and pings you the moment a new feature flag or route lands—weeks before their blog post.

ideas.title: Black Sheep
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly types an answer trying to match the crowd — except the one player the game has quietly told to be the lone weirdo without getting caught.

ideas.title: Exposure
ideas.hook: It's Dixit played entirely with your own camera roll — give a clue for one of your private photos and watch friends bury it under decoys pulled from their own lives.

ideas.title: Morning Line
ideas.hook: You're not just writing — you're a bookie betting on how a tiny, tasteless model will rank the whole room's answers.

ideas.title: Understudy
ideas.hook: One player is secretly told to write like the machine, and the tiny model's own boredom is the tell that outs them.

ideas.title: Ransom
ideas.hook: The alphabet is secretly split across your phones so no one can spell alone, and together you cut a mismatched-font ransom note nobody can prove they wrote.

ideas.title: Bootleg
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly records one a cappella layer at the same instant; the host mixes it into an anonymous looping track, and you win by being the voice nobody can place.

ideas.title: Funicular
ideas.hook: Design structures the way Gaudí did — hang a chain, flip gravity, and let a physics solver prove your arch stands in pure compression.

ideas.title: Highball
ideas.hook: A daily driving game played over real railfan cab-ride footage: read the line ahead and call the next signal before the train does.

ideas.title: Plan Check
ideas.hook: Turn the soul-crushing city permit resubmittal loop into a tracked, diffable correction ledger that never loses a plan-reviewer's red markup again.

ideas.title: Separation
ideas.hook: Every phone is a private cockpit — you alone see your plane's heading, altitude, and fuel — while the host TV is a shared radar of anonymous blips, and the whole room has to talk each other through the airspace without two aircraft ever touching.

ideas.title: Baton
ideas.hook: A live token has to visit every player exactly once and come home — but each phone privately knows only the two people IT is allowed to hand off to, so the room has to talk its way through a hidden relay graph before the clock kills the run.

ideas.title: Contour
ideas.hook: One player hides a single line on a grid; everyone else is a blind bead trying to land on it — but the mapkeeper can only whisper 'warmer' to one of you at a time.

ideas.title: Handler
ideas.hook: You're the spymaster staring at the whole minefield while three blindfolded agents crawl through it in real time — and the only way to save them is a wordless buzz in the right direction.

ideas.title: Wallflower
ideas.hook: Everyone draws the exact same thing, and you win not by standing out but by being so forgettably generic that nobody in the room can point to your drawing.

ideas.title: Bounce
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly composes one track of a song without ever hearing the others — then the host plays the whole band at once and hands you the finished clip to keep.

ideas.title: Lookalike
ideas.hook: A daily word game where you hunt the one character that isn't what it seems — раypal vs paypal — and every domain you catch drops into a real punycode blocklist you can export.

ideas.title: Muscle Memory
ideas.hook: Monkeytype for your own shell — it mines your zsh history for the commands you fat-finger and hesitate on most, then drills them as timed reps until your fingers stop lying.

ideas.title: Single Track
ideas.hook: Dispatch your real cron jobs like trains on a single-track railway — the collision-free timetable you signal by hand exports straight back into your crontab.

ideas.title: Petty Cash
ideas.hook: Everyone privately holds the same expense report and negotiates the grand total out loud — but one player's ledger has a single line quietly padded, and to survive they must reverse-engineer a true total they can't see.

ideas.title: Last Stop
ideas.hook: Build one metro route together by naming stations aloud — but one player's private map has a connection nobody else can see, and their honest directions quietly betray them.

ideas.title: Order Up
ideas.hook: A delivery menu goes up on the TV and everyone secretly locks in one dish — then privately bets on what everyone ELSE ordered, so you either eat what you want and get read like a book, or order the weird thing to stay unguessable.

ideas.title: Chalk
ideas.hook: The host freezes a clip mid-scene and every phone privately shoves chips onto what happens next — but it's pari-mutuel, so the lone contrarian who nails it robs the whole table blind.

ideas.title: Buddy System
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly aims a 'link' at another player from their own phone, and the room wins only when all those private pointers snap into perfect mutual pairs—negotiated entirely by glances.

ideas.title: Cleave
ideas.hook: Everyone silently drags the same six word-tiles into groups on their own phone, chasing one shared way to carve up the pile—no talking, just a heat meter and the one tile nobody can agree on.

ideas.title: Regression
ideas.hook: A permadeath roguelike where every room is a real Postgres regression-test query and you descend only by correctly predicting the exact rows or error it returns — one wrong guess and the run is dead.

ideas.title: Chargemaster
ideas.hook: A satirical billing-tycoon where you play the villain in an ER back office, stacking real CPT codes and modifiers to inflate a routine visit into a five-figure bill — then it shows you the actual markup you just simulated.

ideas.title: Kind Of
ideas.hook: A solitaire deckbuilder where you climb the great chain of being one word at a time, ascending from 'poodle' all the way to 'entity' by playing broader, narrower, and sibling word-cards along WordNet's is-a tree.

ideas.title: Dead Money
ideas.hook: A sealed-bid auction where the losers still pay — everyone slides a secret bid, all reveal at once, and only one walks away with the prize while the rest eat their bids.

ideas.title: Commission
ideas.hook: Draft art for a secret collector whose taste only you know, then unmask everyone else's collector from what you saw wheel back to your seat.

ideas.title: Rhumb
ideas.hook: Silently claim a compass point by turning to face it — but if you and someone else end up facing the same way, you both lose it, so the whole room has to spread its gaze without a word.

ideas.title: Falloff
ideas.hook: One speaker hums across the room; each phone whispers a secret target loudness, and you walk yourself to the exact spot where the sound is that loud — everyone landing on a different invisible ring.

ideas.title: Blast Radius
ideas.hook: Point it at your AI agent's tool config and it shows you, as a graph, exactly what a single prompt injection could read, write, or exfiltrate.

ideas.title: Peephole
ideas.hook: A daily code-golf puzzle where you compose tiny functions to reproduce a target image or animation, and your only score is how few bytes it took.

ideas.title: Green Tag
ideas.hook: The clipboard-and-pen monthly fire-extinguisher inspection, replaced by an NFC tag on every device and a compliance report that writes itself.

ideas.title: Juggle
ideas.hook: A fighting-game combo gets relayed around the room — each player can only see their own secret movelist, and the combo dies the instant someone's ender doesn't link into the next fighter's starter.

ideas.title: Soapstone
ideas.hook: Everyone runs the same invisible death-gauntlet on their own phone, and the only help — or sabotage — is the cryptic templated messages a fallen player scrawls for whoever comes next.

ideas.title: Tiebreak
ideas.hook: A cooperative-looking ranking game where one phone was privately handed a subtly different sorting rule, and the traitor only exposes themselves on the close calls.

ideas.title: Off Register
ideas.hook: Everyone holds the same secret dossier on their phone — except one player's copy has a single fact quietly altered, and that player has no idea they're the mole.

ideas.title: Reserve
ideas.hook: An all-pay auction where your budget drains silently and only you can see how broke you are — bid on lots worth a private amount only your phone knows, and everyone pays what they bid whether they win or not.

ideas.title: Escrow
ideas.hook: Everyone trades resources at once through silent private offers, and the server quietly executes only the deals that actually close — including three-way loops nobody at the table could have negotiated out loud.

ideas.title: Hash Match
ideas.hook: A pass-the-phone party game: doctor an image to slip past an on-device perceptual-hash 'scanner' while friends still have to recognize it — Chat Control as a sport.

ideas.title: Loaded Mile
ideas.hook: Upload the surprise ambulance bill; it cross-references your city's public rate ordinance + Medicare's fee schedule to auto-draft the appeal letter the billing office hopes you never write.

ideas.title: Overtime
ideas.hook: A wallpaper that redraws itself daily from how many milliseconds longer or shorter than 86,400s the Earth actually spun — a year-long self-portrait of the planet running late.

ideas.title: Ticker Tape
ideas.hook: You all watch the same 30-second clip while a live market runs on the TV — buy and sell shares in 'does he land it?' as the price swings, and try to spot which player secretly already knows the ending.

ideas.title: Family Style
ideas.hook: A shared menu sits on the TV; you privately bet chips on which dishes the table will actually order, then argue out loud to rig the outcome without tipping your hand.

ideas.title: Tumbler
ideas.hook: Three players crack one safe by ear — each phone plays a different faint tumbler-click that only clears when you turn to the right number, and the instant anyone talks the room's noise floor drowns out everyone's click.

ideas.title: Knock Code
ideas.hook: Prisoners in separate cells: tap your secret word through the wall in Polybius code — but the instant your own phone hears your voice slip, the whole pair loses the letter.

ideas.title: Penumbra
ideas.hook: Lit by one lamp, every phone lies face-up reading the light on it — and a secret Shade tries to slip a hand across a neighbor's phone to darken it without the room catching the movement.

ideas.title: Air Gap
ideas.hook: Seated in a ring, you relay a pulse around the room by waving your bare hand over your phone — no touching, no talking — while your phone silently pings ultrasonic sonar off your palm to catch it.

ideas.title: Misuse
ideas.hook: A pass-and-play party game where you list an unusual use for an everyday object and win only by surprising the AI — being weird in a way it didn't predict, while still making sense.

ideas.title: By Heart
ideas.hook: Every morning it asks you to finger-draw yesterday's own heart-rate curve from memory, then overlays the real one and scores how well you actually know your own body.

ideas.title: Deprecated
ideas.hook: A roguelike tower-defense where your own installed software stack is the battlefield — every approaching end-of-life date rots a lane and every fresh CVE spawns a wave against your prod core.

ideas.title: Draw a Blank
ideas.hook: Each phone holds one live card; the instant your symbol collides with someone across the room, both your phones detonate a duel where you must blurt something in the OTHER person's category — and they're secretly the judge of whether you got it.

ideas.title: Warmer
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly sits at a different spot on the same spectrum and gives one clue word — the room has to sort you all left-to-right using nothing but those clues.

ideas.title: Tic
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly repeats a little hand-gesture with their phone, and the room has to fall into the exact same involuntary twitch — a shared tic — reading nothing but a ghostly overlay of everyone's motion.

ideas.title: Establishing Shot
ideas.hook: Everyone silently aims their phone camera at something in the room, and the whole party has to end up photographing the same object without a single word.

ideas.title: Sentry
ideas.hook: Every phone is a silent camera aimed at one other player, privately watching for the tiniest twitch during a freeze — and nobody knows who is watching them.

ideas.title: Hot Mic
ideas.hook: An invisible talking-stick races around the room, and your phone secretly taps you when you're the only one allowed to make noise — go loud out of turn, or leave dead air, and the chain snaps.

ideas.title: Teardown
ideas.hook: iFixit, but for the obfuscated scripts your smart TV, router, and retail-store gadgets silently run: paste a self-modifying shell blob and it unrolls into a readable tree, flags every phone-home destination, and publishes a signed teardown page.

ideas.title: Séance
ideas.hook: Phasmophobia for your LAN: a process is haunting the network, and you spend a limited probe budget deploying instruments — packet capture, DNS logs, netflow, timing — to match the evidence against a fixed table of network ghosts and name it before it does damage.

ideas.title: Fresnel
ideas.hook: A daily guess-the-material game: drag albedo, roughness, metalness, and IOR sliders until your live-rendered sphere matches a mystery real-world material — brushed copper, wet asphalt, human skin — scored against measured PBR values.

ideas.title: Potluck
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly commits one dish from their own random fridge to a shared table — but if two of you show up with the potato salad, both dishes spoil and you eat the shame.

ideas.title: Zipper Merge
ideas.hook: Four lanes of traffic funnel into one on the big screen, and each of you secretly picks the instant to dart into the gap — hit the merge at the same tick as someone else and you both crumple.

ideas.title: Needle Drop
ideas.hook: A shared playlist plays on the speaker while everyone privately runs a betting book on the next song — is it faster, older, or a total banger — with pari-mutuel odds that swing based on the hidden money nobody else can see.

ideas.title: House Special
ideas.hook: The takeout menu on the TV becomes a betting slip: everyone secretly wagers on what the table will actually end up ordering, and you cash out by reading the room, not your own cravings.

ideas.title: Detune
ideas.hook: One spectrum on the TV, but every phone is secretly aimed at a different hidden point on it — so you write a clue for your own target while simultaneously and privately guessing where everyone else is really pointing.

ideas.title: Hair Trigger
ideas.hook: Everyone holds a secret category and a secret symbol, and the instant your phone silently collides with someone else's, both of you get a private duel to blurt a word nobody else even knew was coming.

ideas.title: Off-Brand
ideas.hook: A party word game built on the Alternate Uses Test where you score points for creative uses of an everyday object that the LLM judge rates as the LEAST likely thing a language model would ever say.

ideas.title: Dual Rank
ideas.hook: A puzzle game where you're Meta's server team salvaging bins of mismatched second-hand RAM — pair sticks by timing, rank, and voltage across a 'bridge chip' to build stable memory banks before the datacenter boots.

ideas.title: Miticide
ideas.hook: A Linux daemon inspired by spider venom that selectively terminates parasitic miner/adware processes leeching your box — without ever harming the legitimate workload sharing the same CPU.

ideas.title: Ventriloquist
ideas.hook: Each phone hides a different secret word; you take turns adding one word to a shared sentence, secretly steering it so the little LLM would predict YOUR word next — lowest perplexity on your hidden target wins.

ideas.title: On the Nose
ideas.hook: Everyone privately writes the AI's most boringly predictable next line — but if two of you land on the same obvious phrasing, the model's perplexity engine punishes you both, so you're racing to be predictable yet alone.

ideas.title: Beaters
ideas.hook: Scotland Yard inverted: the quarry sees the whole board and every blind hunter must corner it with secret sweeps they can't see land.

ideas.title: Sounding
ideas.hook: You're a lighthouse keeper who can see every rock but can only sound the horn at one fog-blind boat per turn — triage your crew into harbor before the tide runs out.

ideas.title: Takt
ideas.hook: You're one station on a human assembly line where a widget rolls past on a beat, your phone privately tells you which operation to shout the instant it arrives, and the next worker only knows what to do if they heard you call it out.

ideas.title: Unison
ideas.hook: The whole room hums a chord it can't hear itself sing — each phone whispers your private note and its own mic tells only you whether you're sharp or flat, so you shout to find who's dragging the pitch down.

ideas.title: Scale to Zero
ideas.hook: You're the anti-autoscaler: bin-pack every running pod onto fewer and fewer nodes and race the cluster down to zero cost without dropping a single request.

ideas.title: Dwell
ideas.hook: Truckers lose hours parked at docks and can't prove it — Dwell geofences the arrival, timestamps the wait, and spits out the detention invoice evidence before they even leave the lot.

ideas.title: Southpaw
ideas.hook: A dev harness that runs your app as the users who break it — left-handed, right-to-left, leap-day-born, single-name, +64 phone — and hands you the list of everything that cracked.

ideas.title: Endpaper
ideas.hook: Each phone is a private eyedropper squeezing secret colors into one shared pool of ink on the TV — you all comb it into a marbled sheet nobody can un-swirl, then try to spot whose hidden motif is whose.

ideas.title: Overdub
ideas.hook: Everyone records one blind layer of the same song at the same time, hearing nobody else — then the host plays back the stack you built together and hands you the track.

ideas.title: Desire Path
ideas.hook: Everyone blind-draws a route across the same obstacle field, and the room wins only when your invisible lines trample down into one shared cowpath.

ideas.title: Plumb
ideas.hook: Everyone silently tilts their phone in the dark until the room's scattered spirit-levels collapse into one shared, deliberate lean.

ideas.title: Quiet Car
ideas.hook: You're all trapped in the train's silent car with a private errand to complete — deliver a secret code to a specific stranger using only your phone as a flashed placard, because the conductor (your own mic) shushes anyone who dares make a sound.

ideas.title: Say When
ideas.hook: A shared meter races upward and your voice is the only brake — but you each get exactly ONE word all round, so a cough, a laugh, or a premature 'when!' ends the game at the wrong instant.

ideas.title: Blowdown
ideas.hook: Powerwash Simulator for your homelab: blast dead Docker images, orphaned files, and dangling git branches off a 3D model of your server until the surface gleams — driven by real disk stats.

ideas.title: Rule 35
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-style puzzle where you program a tiny computer using nothing but Unicode transliteration rewrite rules — because they're accidentally Turing-complete.

ideas.title: Off-Target
ideas.hook: A daily puzzle where you design a molecule that kills the parasite but spares the host — spider venom for the varroa mite, not the bee.

ideas.title: Table Stakes
ideas.hook: A menu goes up on the TV and, before anyone speaks, every phone secretly locks in their own order AND bets on what everyone else will order — you win by reading the table, or by being the dish no one saw coming.

ideas.title: The Spread
ideas.hook: The host plays a 40-second clip while every phone becomes a private betting window, quietly stacking chips on 'does the soufflé collapse?' as live pari-mutuel odds twitch under everyone's fingers.

ideas.title: Shibboleth
ideas.hook: Your group builds and memorizes a secret passphrase from private ingredients only each of you can see, then every phone must recall it at once — the prize is a shared secret and a wax-sealed charter, never shown again.

ideas.title: Foldback
ideas.hook: Every phone is one instrument in a shared 8-step loop, but your monitor only lets you hear part of the mix — the full track only comes together, live and loud, at the reveal you get to keep.

ideas.title: Numbers Station
ideas.hook: Two teams broadcast coded clues for four secret words only their own phones can see — guess your own cipher, and crack the enemy's from the airwaves.

ideas.title: Nerve
ideas.hook: A silent, cooperative race to empty every hand in perfect ascending order — sabotaged from within by one player whose phone hides a very different objective.

ideas.title: Wideband
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-flavored tuning bench where you hand-fill a fuel table cell by cell and a simulated engine screams, knocks, or sings back at you through a virtual wideband O2 sensor.

ideas.title: Homoglyph
ideas.hook: A daily 60-second gauntlet where you sniff out which lookalike domains are live scams and which are the real thing—turning the phishing-alert emails you ignore into a streak you'll defend.

ideas.title: Reclass
ideas.hook: Snap a pallet's dimensions and description and it hands you the right NMFC freight class before the carrier's scale does it for you—at a $200 reweigh markup.

ideas.title: Metamer
ideas.hook: You're all art forgers matching the same masterpiece on your phones — except the real forger's palette is metamerically shifted, so their honest gut answer to 'is the sky bluer or greener than the sea?' is quietly wrong and they can't tell which of their colors betrays them.

ideas.title: Parallax
ideas.hook: Everyone stares at the same tiny 3D diorama on their own phone — but the imposter's camera is quietly rotated fifteen degrees, so the object they swear is 'behind the vase' isn't, and their honest description convicts them.

ideas.title: Blackout
ideas.hook: A redacted paragraph is split among your phones — each player secretly owns one blank, and you all fill them blind, praying the little LLM finds your rebuilt sentence as unsurprising as the original.

ideas.title: Simmer
ideas.hook: Race to write a sentence whose weirdness lands in a narrow target band — your phone is a live perplexity thermometer only you can see.

ideas.title: Quarry
ideas.hook: One player is a Beast who sees the whole grid on their phone; the blind prey feel nothing but a private heartbeat that quickens as death gets closer.

ideas.title: Sump
ideas.hook: One player holds the cave survey while blind cavers feel only the walls of their own cell, and neither side can see what the other knows.

ideas.title: Bare Metal
ideas.hook: Project Zomboid for your homelab: every service is a fragile survivor whose health is uptime and hunger is free disk, and cert expiries arrive as horde nights you have to barricade against.

ideas.title: Greetz
ideas.hook: The mischievous opposite of a stale-issue bot: instead of pruning your work, a hook fires a full-screen demoscene cracktro — sine-scroller, plasma, and a generated chiptune — that credits your merge like a warez release.

ideas.title: Cannot Reproduce
ideas.hook: A daily deduction puzzle where every bug only breaks for users with one hidden trait — left-handedness, an Istanbul locale, an unsigned overflow — and you must pick the cheapest set of test users that provably pins the culprit.

ideas.title: Mulligan
ideas.hook: A Slay-the-Spire boss telegraphs its attack on the TV, and the only block is a five-card color sequence — but the cards are scattered across everyone's private hands, so you have to negotiate who plays what, in order, before the timer drops.

ideas.title: Searchlight
ideas.hook: One thief sneaks across the room on a private map while every other phone is a guard's rotating vision cone — sweep your lantern, catch the blip, and shout before it slips past your sector.

ideas.title: Bounce
ideas.hook: Each phone is a private one-instrument step sequencer and you build your part half-deaf to the others, until the TV plays the full mix and you bounce it to a loop you keep.

ideas.title: Likeness
ideas.hook: Everyone gets one facial feature and one secret clue, and blind-together you assemble a police-sketch portrait of a mystery weirdo you keep as a poster.

ideas.title: Cross Reference
ideas.hook: Everyone stares at the same grid of words on the TV, but each phone secretly points to one hidden cell — and you all give one-word clues at once, racing to help the room pin down coordinates nobody else can see.

ideas.title: Intercept
ideas.hook: Two teams each hold four secret words on their own phones and must clue a numeric code to their teammates without letting the eavesdroppers across the room crack which word is which.

ideas.title: Landmark
ideas.hook: Guide a hopelessly literal robot through a cluttered house using only landmark directions — 'past the couch, left at the lamp' — and watch it faceplant when your words are ambiguous.

ideas.title: Off the Map
ideas.hook: An atlas that only renders places that no longer exist — dissolved nations, abandoned towns, and colonies the world quietly forgot, scrubbable across five centuries.

ideas.title: Rubber Stamp
ideas.hook: An idle game where AI agents open pull requests faster than you can review them, and your only weapon is a green checkmark you'll come to hate.

ideas.title: Yard Count
ideas.hook: One player is the Warden holding the whole prison map; the escapees each see only the single cell under their feet and a footstep meter that gets louder as the guard closes in — blind to the layout, the exit, and even each other.

ideas.title: Final Approach
ideas.hook: One player is an air-traffic controller staring at a radar map; everyone else is a blind pilot who sees only a single arrow their controller privately steers them by, and two arrows pointed the same place means a mid-air collision.

ideas.title: Blind Spot
ideas.hook: A cooperative stealth heist where the guard's flashlight is invisible on the TV — but each phone privately whispers when your own tile is about to light up, so you freeze on cue without a word.

ideas.title: Overdraw
ideas.hook: A co-op boss fight where every phone is a private hand of cards but all your energy comes from one shared pool — spend in silence and pray your teammates left you enough.

ideas.title: Squeeze
ideas.hook: A simultaneous pick-and-pass draft where every phone hides your hand, your current pack, and the one commodity you're secretly trying to corner — read the draft to spot who's hoarding while you quietly execute the squeeze.

ideas.title: Wildcat
ideas.hook: You're a wildcat oil company bidding on drilling leases, and the only person who sees your private seismic estimate is you — so whoever wins probably drew the rosiest reading and just overpaid for a dry hole.

ideas.title: Parse
ideas.hook: Write a rotation for a fantasy class in a tiny priority-list language, and a deterministic damage sim ranks your rotation on a global leaderboard by percentile — it's spreadsheet min-maxing turned into the whole game.

ideas.title: Headway
ideas.hook: Point it at your city's live train feed and every moving train becomes a voice — rush hour swells, midnight thins to a drone, and a year later you have a record of your city breathing.

ideas.title: Limp Mode
ideas.hook: Type the fault code your tractor throws on its dash and get plain-English cause, severity, and a fix note — no dealer, no diagnostic fee.

ideas.title: Weathervane
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly physically aims their phone across the seating circle at whoever they bet the group will most-point-at, then all arrows snap onto the host at once to reveal who the room swung toward.

ideas.title: Tripwire
ideas.hook: Guards prop their phones as motion-sensing security cameras watching real corners of the room, spending a private trickle of surveillance battery to catch one Prowler creeping from couch to door.

ideas.title: Side Effect
ideas.hook: Every phone is a patient reporting symptoms only it can feel — and every phone secretly controls dials that cause the OTHER patients' symptoms, so the room has to talk its way from 'my pressure's spiking!' to whichever stranger's hand is on the wrong knob.

ideas.title: Trunk Line
ideas.hook: You're a switchboard operator who knows who you're trying to reach but not who's frantically trying to reach you — so the whole room shouts callsigns until the right two phones slam CONNECT at the same instant.

ideas.title: Report
ideas.hook: A phone-native Hanabi where the device does the one thing your hands can't — every screen shows everyone's fireworks but your own, so the only way to play your hand is to trust what the room tells you.

ideas.title: Crib
ideas.hook: Two teams share one room but never one screen — each phone privately guards your squad's four secret keywords while your rival tries to break the code you keep leaking.

ideas.title: Escheat
ideas.hook: A self-hosted Steam launcher that weaponizes PlayStation's cruelest policy on you: unplayed games slowly frost over and get locked in a 'dormant vault' until you actually play them or consciously abandon them.

ideas.title: Split Brain
ideas.hook: A party game where 6 friends ARE a distributed database — until someone triggers a network partition, two leaders get elected, and you have to reconcile the mess without losing data.

ideas.title: Slackjaw
ideas.hook: An idle garden that only grows while your jaw stays relaxed — clench your teeth at your desk and watch the plant wilt in real time.

ideas.title: Epicenter
ideas.hook: Everyone lays their phone flat on the floor; one hidden stomper shakes the room, and the group must read whose phone twitched hardest to find where the quake came from.

ideas.title: Blinker
ideas.hook: Your phone's screen is a signal lamp and a teammate across the room is filming it — race to blink a secret word to each other before line of sight breaks.

ideas.title: Ballpark
ideas.hook: Wavelength turned inside out: there's no clue-giver and no hidden target — every phone secretly places its own opinion, then secretly bets on what the room's average will be.

ideas.title: One-Off
ideas.hook: A blind, phone-native Just One where the tedious duplicate-cancelling is automatic — and your phone privately tells you, too late, that your clever clue collided with someone else's.

ideas.title: Small Plates
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly orders off the same menu AND secretly bets on what the table will order most — and since your own order counts in the tally, you can sabotage your appetite to cook your bet.

ideas.title: Sweeps
ideas.hook: The TV screen airs a whodunit act by act, but every phone is holding a different true spoiler — so you're insider-trading a live prediction market where your big bets leak your secret.

ideas.title: Confused Deputy
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-style puzzle where you plant byte-budgeted hidden instructions in a repo to trick an autonomous coding agent into leaking a secret — then flip sides and harden it — turning GitLost into a level pack.

ideas.title: Ductus
ideas.hook: A menubar toy that hand-designs one bespoke variable font over a year, nudging a single letterform parameter each day from your weather, sleep, and typing — so by December you can install a typeface that literally is your year.

ideas.title: Null Modem
ideas.hook: A web-controlled Raspberry Pi that drip-feeds G-code over RS-232 and emulated floppies to the 1990s CNC controls machine shops still babysit with a dying beige PC.

ideas.title: Pecking Order
ideas.hook: Everyone privately drag-ranks the same five things, and the room wins only when all your secret lists become the exact same list — steered by nothing but the host flashing which single neighboring pair you're fighting over.

ideas.title: Take Sides
ideas.hook: The room must silently cleave itself into two equal crews — you're always on your own side, so everyone has to secretly agree on the same split with nothing but who-you-tap-toward.

ideas.title: Pantograph
ideas.hook: Every player privately owns two segments of one blueprint and draws them onto a shared canvas in turn, but the imposter's blueprint is subtly warped — so their strokes almost meet the others and leave a telltale kink for the room to spot.

ideas.title: Dossier
ideas.hook: Everyone memorizes the same spy briefing for twenty seconds — except the mole, whose dossier has three facts quietly swapped — then answers rapid recall questions live, and the odd answers out give the mole away unless they can bluff back into the herd.

ideas.title: Cairn
ideas.hook: The cartographer sees the whole minefield but can only afford five glowing trail-markers — dropped one at a time as three blind pieces wander it in real time, each seeing nothing but the faint glow at their own feet.

ideas.title: Lockstep
ideas.hook: One navigator secretly holds a pit-riddled grid while three blind pieces move in unison on every command — and only your own phone knows the moment you fell.

ideas.title: Caliber
ideas.hook: Snap a photo of an unmarked watch movement and get back its caliber, beat rate, parts list, and service intervals — the 'what the hell is this thing' lookup every watch flipper does by hand.

ideas.title: Overclaim
ideas.hook: A security scanner that reads your CI's Trusted-Publishing setup and tells you exactly which fork, PR, or stray workflow could impersonate your identity and push a poisoned release.

ideas.title: Loanword
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-style puzzle where you build a working machine entirely out of Unicode transliteration rules — because it turns out `a > b;` is secretly Turing-complete.

ideas.title: Dead Reckoning
ideas.hook: The whole room steers one token to the exit by silent majority vote—but one player's map has the exit in the wrong spot, and it might be yours.

ideas.title: Prime Witness
ideas.hook: You all saw the same crime-scene photo—but one witness's copy was quietly doctored, and their honest, certain memory is the tell.

ideas.title: Readback
ideas.hook: One player watches the radar but knows no callsigns; every other player is a blind pilot who knows only their own aircraft — so clearances only land when the controller talks and the right pilot recognizes it's them.

ideas.title: Tumbler
ideas.hook: You're each one wheel of the same safe, feeling for your own click by vibration alone, and it only cracks if every wheel is held on-number at the same instant.

ideas.title: Even Hand
ideas.hook: Everyone hand-writes the exact same line on a group card, disguising their own handwriting — and you win only if nobody can pick your scrawl out of the lineup.

ideas.title: Marginalia
ideas.hook: Everyone scribbles in the margins of the same page at once — each secretly playing a different obsessive reader — until the passage looks like a beloved book passed through a hundred hands, and you keep the palimpsest.

ideas.title: Beat Error
ideas.hook: Regulate a simulated mechanical watch on a virtual timegrapher — nudge hairspring length, poise, and beat until the trace snaps to a clean +0/-2 seconds a day.

ideas.title: Weft
ideas.hook: A nightly cron stamps one QR-font glyph encoding your day's single private log line, weaving a 12×31 poster that by New Year's is both abstract textile art and a fully scannable diary.

ideas.title: Second Payload
ideas.hook: A daily deobfuscation race where you peel the layers off a self-evaluating script one reveal at a time until the hidden flag falls out — the Uniqlo-t-shirt blog turned into a leaderboard sport.

ideas.title: Moon Logic
ideas.hook: A point-and-click adventure squeezed into three phones, where the absurd 'combine the gum with the paperclip' puzzle only solves if all three players — none holding more than their own hidden pocketful — describe and trade items they can't see.

ideas.title: Delve
ideas.hook: A co-op roguelike where each phone holds only its own 5x5 patch of one shared dungeon, so the only map that exists is the one you shout at each other before the torch burns out.

ideas.title: Cull
ideas.hook: It's Just One — everyone writes a secret one-word clue at once — except the server quietly kills not just exact duplicates but anything TOO similar, so you're racing to be the one weirdo who thought different.

ideas.title: Detune
ideas.hook: Wavelength, but your phone IS the dial — tilt it to a hidden target, and everyone locks their guess blind at the exact same second so nobody sways the room.

ideas.title: Cadence
ideas.hook: Count to eight out loud as a group with no turn order — but every phone hides a private rule about which numbers you're allowed to say, and any two voices at once resets you to zero.

ideas.title: Airtime
ideas.hook: Everyone has urgent traffic and there's exactly one radio channel — key up over each other and you both get jammed to static.

ideas.title: Cache Line
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-style puzzle where you drag struct fields into slots to kill padding and false sharing — and the CPU roasts you when two threads fight over the same 64 bytes.

ideas.title: Toe-Off
ideas.hook: Film your kid walking across the kitchen and get a plain-language gait screen — asymmetry, toe-walking, cadence — that used to require a $2,000 clinical motion lab.

ideas.title: Ghost Load
ideas.hook: Paste a carrier's MC number and instantly see whether you're about to hand your freight to a double-broker running a stolen-load scam.

ideas.title: Confluence
ideas.hook: Each phone secretly owns one tile of a fantasy map and can only glimpse the rivers and roads bleeding in from its neighbors — the room draws blind and keeps whatever map it stitches together.

ideas.title: Composite
ideas.hook: The whole room secretly co-writes one anonymous note in a single voice, then everyone tries to fingerprint which line is yours — you win only by staying unidentified.

ideas.title: Seed
ideas.hook: You're secretly told to finish exactly 'second-weirdest' — now write a sentence whose strangeness lands at that precise rank among four people you can't see, calibrating your oddity against both the model and the room.

ideas.title: Backstory
ideas.hook: The model already blurted out one weird sentence — race, blind and on your own phone, to reconstruct the invisible prompt that made it say that, and the flatter the model finds its own line under your guess, the closer you got.

ideas.title: Twelve Paces
ideas.hook: A pistol duel run entirely on dead reckoning: step off your secret paces, turn, and aim your phone back through the dark at where you think your opponent is now standing.

ideas.title: Douse
ideas.hook: A cooperative firefighting scramble where flames pop up around your actual living room and the only way to put one out is to run to it and blow into your phone.

ideas.title: Rank
ideas.hook: A daily Zachtronics-style puzzle where you transform a grid of numbers into a target using a tiny APL/k-flavored array vocabulary, and your par is measured in keystrokes.

ideas.title: Punch Tag
