ideas.hook: Replace the paper punch-tags dangling off every fire extinguisher with a QR sticker, so a tech's monthly walkthrough becomes a scan-and-go audit trail that survives a fire marshal's visit.

ideas.title: Reclass
ideas.hook: Snap a pallet's dims and weight and get the correct density-based NMFC freight class plus BOL-ready text — before the carrier reweighs you and slaps on a reclass fee.

ideas.title: Bleed
ideas.hook: A silent all-pay auction where your dwindling bankroll and your private worth for each lot live only on your phone — you bid, and whether you win or lose, everyone pays.

ideas.title: Magpie
ideas.hook: A booster draft with the cardboard removed: your pack, your hoard, and your secret scoring goal all live privately on your phone, packs rotate on a shared tick, and nobody stalls the table.

ideas.title: Family Style
ideas.hook: The takeout menu becomes a market: privately bet how many of you secretly want each dish, then cash in for reading the table's appetite better than they read themselves.

ideas.title: Overround
ideas.hook: Turn whatever's playing on the TV into a live parimutuel betting floor where you get rich by out-reading the room, not the show.

ideas.title: Four-Way
ideas.hook: You're all cars nosing into a one-lane intersection and your phone whispers only YOUR secret urgency — ambulance, gas leak, running late — so you have to bark your priority across the table and thread the crossing one car at a time before somebody T-bones.

ideas.title: Venn
ideas.hook: Each phone hides a different fistful of words with exactly one you ALL secretly share — find it by shouting your lists across the table, then scream it in unison before the ring collapses.

ideas.title: Ride Along
ideas.hook: Watches the code your AI agent writes for you and quietly mints spaced-repetition flashcards from it, so the stuff that shipped without you understanding it doesn't stay a black box.

ideas.title: Sampler
ideas.hook: A daemon that stitches one row of a generative embroidery pattern per day from your life-data, so by New Year's you can export a real cross-stitch chart of your entire year and thread it by hand.

ideas.title: Drill
ideas.hook: Wraps your scariest long-running homelab jobs in a PAYDAY 2 safe-drill: the progress bar can jam, and you have to run over and babysit it before the guards (alerts) close in.

ideas.title: Crosstalk
ideas.hook: Every phone privately cycles its own secret category, and the instant two players' hidden shapes collide, both buzz and race to shout an answer in the other's category — multiple duels erupting across the room at once.

ideas.title: Off-Model
ideas.hook: Six players draw the same secret word at once, blind on their own phones — but one got a sneakily different word, and the gallery reveal is a lineup to find the fake.

ideas.title: Prix Fixe
ideas.hook: A menu prediction market — everyone secretly picks what they'd actually order AND privately bets on which dish the table will crown, so you win by modeling your friends' cravings instead of your own.

ideas.title: Sweat
ideas.hook: A live in-play betting layer over any short clip you're all watching — the game isn't the outcome, it's whether you hold your nerve or bail for a partial payout while a rival maybe already cashed out.

ideas.title: Unison
ideas.hook: Each phone is a private four-pad drum loop and the TV plays them all stacked at once, so the room must silently sculpt four secret patterns into a single clean beat by ear alone.

ideas.title: Carbon Copy
ideas.hook: Everyone blindly draws the same squiggle at once, then chases a ghostly average of the room until four strangers' scribbles snap into one shape.

ideas.title: Deadfall
ideas.hook: One phone is a grid only the Trapper sees, secretly seeding pits; the prey creep across blind, each feeling only a private, maddeningly vague 'something's close' buzz.

ideas.title: Portage
ideas.hook: One phone is the only map of a pitch-dark cavern; the Guide is muted and can spotlight just one blind Porter per tick, so someone is always stumbling in the dark.

ideas.title: Continuity Error
ideas.hook: Two of you are script supervisors watching the same scene on different phones, and the only way to catch what changed between takes is to describe your screen out loud fast enough to point at it together.

ideas.title: Keyed Up
ideas.hook: Everyone shares one voice channel that can only carry a single talker, so filing your ticking backlog of incidents means grabbing the mic before someone steps on you.

ideas.title: Slalom
ideas.hook: Each phone secretly owns a few word-slots in one shared sentence and fills them completely blind, and you all win only if the Frankenstein sentence you assemble lands the model's perplexity needle inside a narrow green gate — too coherent fails, too random fails.

ideas.title: Cold Read
ideas.hook: Stop guessing the right word and start guessing the boring word — everyone privately races to name what a dumb little language model thinks comes next, and picking the same answer as your neighbor splits the pot.

ideas.title: Bottleneck
ideas.hook: A Satisfactory-style conveyor-belt factory view of your homelab where each service is a machine, real bytes/sec is belt throughput, and belts visibly clog and back up when something stalls.

ideas.title: Severance
ideas.hook: Lethal Company's escalating scrap-quota grafted onto your junk drawer: your house is the moon, every item gets appraised against real eBay sold prices, and if you don't hit this week's rising declutter quota, the Company lets you go.

ideas.title: Under Construction
ideas.hook: A cron builds one tiny brutalist webpage per day from your activity and links them prev/next like a 90s webring, so by New Year's you've hand-woven a 365-page site of your life that's forever, deliberately, under construction.

ideas.title: Watering Hole
ideas.hook: Every round you privately slip through one of four identical doors and only feel how crowded YOUR door is — herd blind until the whole room piles into one.

ideas.title: Murmur
ideas.hook: You're each handed one secret neighbor to color-match; chase them and the whole hidden ring gossips its way into a single hue.

ideas.title: Test Audience
ideas.hook: The TV plays a reel of short clips; on each one you privately log your real reaction AND secretly bet on what the room's reaction will be — you win by reading the crowd, not the screen.

ideas.title: Two-Top
ideas.hook: The TV shows a restaurant menu; one hidden diner has secretly ordered, and the rest of you are a betting syndicate placing chips on their order — each of you holding a different private tip about what they'll pick.

ideas.title: Compound
ideas.hook: Every phone secretly holds one half of a word — FIRE, or -FLY — and the room becomes a shouting trading floor where you audio-hunt the stranger whose half completes a real word before the reactor melts down.

ideas.title: Number Station
ideas.hook: The room must count aloud from one to twenty, but each phone secretly owns only a scattered handful of the numbers — so you track the climbing count by ear and know it's your voice from a ledger nobody else can see.

ideas.title: Graze
ideas.hook: A bullet-hell wave fills the TV, but the shots aimed at YOU are invisible to you and to the screen — only your teammates' phones can see them, so survival is one long panicked chorus of 'LEFT! LEFT! DIVE!'

ideas.title: Leak
ideas.hook: Build a secret gauntlet on your own phone, then quietly aim a swarm of creeps down a rival's lane and watch the TV find out who forgot to patch a hole.

ideas.title: Cellmate
ideas.hook: Two prisoners in separate cells must smuggle a secret word to each other in tap code — because the instant either of them says a single word out loud, the guard hears it and the message is lost.

ideas.title: Holdout
ideas.hook: A silence-endurance auction where you win the lot you secretly want by being the last one who never makes a sound — while the people who don't want it heckle you until you crack.

ideas.title: Laugh Track
ideas.hook: Everyone watches the same dumb clip while secretly betting whether the room cracks up or dies — and your own phone's mic is snitching on how hard you're actually laughing.

ideas.title: Kickback
ideas.hook: The table is picking one takeout order together, and you're secretly betting chips on which dish wins while pretending your loud opinions aren't completely rigged.

ideas.title: Skim
ideas.hook: An honest reading receipt that watches how you scroll and quietly tells you 'you actually read 38% of this' — no self-deception allowed.

ideas.title: Roach Motel
ideas.hook: A browser extension that grades every site on how hard it makes leaving — and posts the shadiest cancel-flows to a public wall of shame.

ideas.title: Stoke
ideas.hook: A Don't-Starve campfire lives on your desktop and only survives while YOU physically move — sit too long and the camp goes dark.

ideas.title: Tiptoe
ideas.hook: Everyone has secret chores to finish and a sleeping dragon on the TV that wakes if the room gets loud — so you must negotiate the whole plan without a single word.

ideas.title: Lip Service
ideas.hook: Mouth the secret word to your partner — but the second your throat makes a sound, your own phone rats you out and the point is dead.

ideas.title: Pigeonhole
ideas.hook: Secretly dealt a random word, you race to write the one fill-in-the-blank sentence so airtight that the little language model can only guess a single answer.

ideas.title: Static
ideas.hook: Everyone writes a sentence, but one player is secretly sabotaged — and the little language model's perplexity ranking is the only lie-detector on the table.

ideas.title: Flock
ideas.hook: On each shared pulse the whole room performs a private hand-motion at once, and you silently steer the flock toward all doing the exact same move without ever agreeing on which.

ideas.title: On Three
ideas.hook: Everyone slaps down a face-down symbol at once and the room tries to silently collapse onto a single shared pick as every matched symbol burns off the board forever.

ideas.title: Ignition
ideas.hook: A Jackbox-style party game where everyone holds a piece of the answer but the shared screen only fits three ideas at once — say the wrong thing and someone's clue gets evicted forever.

ideas.title: Backflow
ideas.hook: A one-tap field app that walks a certified tester through a backflow-preventer test and spits out the exact jurisdiction's completed PDF, so nobody transcribes gauge readings onto paper again.

ideas.title: Riprap
ideas.hook: A physics wave-defense puzzle where you stack interlocking concrete armor units to keep a procedurally-storming ocean from smashing your harbor.

ideas.title: Off the Record
ideas.hook: The room co-writes one anonymous confession sheet from your real answers — and you only get to keep it if nobody can prove who wrote which line.

ideas.title: Flipbook
ideas.hook: Three phones each secretly animate a few frames of the same looping GIF, negotiating only the seams in real time — and the finished loop is the keepsake you all keep, no winner.

ideas.title: Snap
ideas.hook: Your phone quietly flips category cards until it collides with someone else's — then only your two phones buzz, and you duel head-to-head while three other duels rage in parallel across the room.

ideas.title: Crib
ideas.hook: Two teams each hold four secret words only their own phones can see, and every clue you shout to your partner is also a crack the enemy is quietly trying to pry open.

ideas.title: Place Card
ideas.hook: Each phone holds a secret map of who's-who around the table — but the imposter's map has two codenames swapped, so when the room points, their finger is quietly, consistently wrong.

ideas.title: House Rules
ideas.hook: Everyone plays the same 60-second card game, but one player's phone quietly shows an inverted rulebook — and their sincere, backwards strategy is the only tell.

ideas.title: Glint
ideas.hook: Point your phone at an office ceiling and it spits out a Unified Glare Rating—turning a $2,000 luminance-meter compliance job into a free snapshot.

ideas.title: Checkout
ideas.hook: A bot-vs-bot arena where you program a shopping bot to snipe limited stock in a simulated sneaker/GPU drop—all the scalper adrenaline, none of the real-world victims.

ideas.title: First Pass
ideas.hook: Feed it your raw home-sequenced DNA and it tells you—entirely offline—how fast you burn caffeine, codeine, and warfarin, no doctor and no cloud involved.

ideas.title: Undertow
ideas.hook: Everyone edits the same sentence, but each phone is secretly chasing a different word — you win by rewriting the setup so the tiny in-browser model would most likely blurt YOUR secret next.

ideas.title: Chalk
ideas.hook: Every phone privately splits a stack of chips onto which word a tiny in-browser language model thinks comes next — and betting the crowd's favorite pays peanuts.

ideas.title: Wipe
ideas.hook: An MMO raid boss on the TV hands each phone a SECRET mechanic — spread, stack, take the tower — and the whole room must physically scramble to the right floor spots before the cast bar fills or everyone wipes.

ideas.title: Undercard
ideas.hook: A phone-native auto-battler where you draft and secretly position a tiny warband from a shared shop, then watch the TV auto-resolve your blind bet against comps you never got to see.

ideas.title: Downbeat
ideas.hook: With no count-in and no shared sound, the whole room tries to tap their phones at the exact same instant — each phone privately whispering, through haptics, whether you tend to rush or drag the group's invisible pulse.

ideas.title: Handshake
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly points a finger at one other player; a pair only clicks into place when the choice is mutual, and the whole room must silently converge on a perfect matching before the clock runs out.

ideas.title: Sidelobe
ideas.hook: A phased-array puzzle where you steer an invisible beam onto targets by tuning each emitter's phase — chasing a tight main lobe while a stray sidelobe threatens to trip the wrong sensor.

ideas.title: Overlock
ideas.hook: The compliance autopilot for self-storage lien auctions — track every delinquent unit's state-specific notice clock, generate legally-timed certified notices, and hand a clean, defensible unit off to online auction.

ideas.title: Overburden
ideas.hook: git bisect reimagined as a Terraria-style mining descent — dig down through layered commit strata, running checks at each seam until you strike the exact commit that buried your regression.

ideas.title: Crawlspace
ideas.hook: A survival-horror hunt on a foggy map where one phone is the thing in the dark seeing everything, and the survivors each see only the room they're standing in — so the terror lives entirely in what your phone won't show you.

ideas.title: Engine
ideas.hook: A deckbuilder where all four players buy, draw, and combo AT ONCE on their own phones — the shared market is the only battlefield, and two players reaching for the same card both go home empty-handed.

ideas.title: Order Up
ideas.hook: A gorgeous diner menu fills the TV, everyone secretly picks what they'd really order, and then you spend the whole game betting chips on what your friends chose — reading their tastes like a bookie reads a horse.

ideas.title: Tote
ideas.hook: A short clip plays on the TV and every phone becomes a private betting window in a live pari-mutuel market where the odds lurch as the room secretly piles chips on 'will he actually open the box?'

ideas.title: Long Tail
ideas.hook: Race to fill one blank with a word the language model finds totally plausible but nobody else in the room thought of — claim your own slice of its imagination.

ideas.title: Hedge
ideas.hook: Bet your chips on exactly where a tiny language model will get confused — read its next-token entropy like a poker tell, one word at a time.

ideas.title: Type Specimen
ideas.hook: A daily hunt through a shared cellular-automata rulespace where the first player to find a stable new creature gets to name it forever.

ideas.title: Effigy
ideas.hook: Instead of hiding your leaked .git directory, serve crawlers a bottomless fake one wired with canary credentials so their own theft rats them out.

ideas.title: Low Bridge
ideas.hook: A trip planner for oversize truck loads that plots a route and screams before you wedge a 13'6" trailer under an 11'8" bridge.

ideas.title: Swell
ideas.hook: The room has to sing one rising-and-falling volume curve together — but only your phone knows which swells are yours to carry and which to shut up for.

ideas.title: Switchboard
ideas.hook: One frantic operator at a plugboard tries to patch three shouting callers to the right line — but every jack looks the same, and they all light up at once.

ideas.title: Clash
ideas.hook: A four-piece band that must fill every beat of a bar with exactly one instrument each — but you tap in private, and landing on the same beat as someone else clangs and voids you both.

ideas.title: Setback
ideas.hook: A blind, real-time land-grab where you greedily grow your plot across a field you can't see — and any patch two players both claim is deleted from BOTH of you.

ideas.title: Quarry
ideas.hook: One player hides a monster only their phone can see and moves it every turn, while the blind hunters each feel a private heartbeat that pounds faster the closer their own token creeps — and they have to drop the net on the same square, together.

ideas.title: Usher
ideas.hook: One player's phone is a pitch-black manor only they can see, and they sweep a lantern that lights just one blind guest's phone at a time — so everyone else must feel their way to the ballroom before the candle gutters.

ideas.title: Anchorage
ideas.hook: A daily prediction market on real cargo ships: bet which of two vessels clears the harbor first using live AIS tracks, and turn idle ship-watching into a bloodsport.

ideas.title: Unlabeled
ideas.hook: A daily 'name that chart' — we strip the title and axis labels off a real published graph and you race the world to guess what it actually plots.

ideas.title: Reclass
ideas.hook: Snap a pallet, get its NMFC freight class in seconds, and dodge the carrier's reweigh-and-reclass surprise fee before your BOL ever ships.

ideas.title: Warden
ideas.hook: One warden watches the whole prison from above while blindfolded inmates grope cell by cell toward the fence — and every tick the warden drops one searchlight on the spot they bet a body is about to be.

ideas.title: Lighthouse
ideas.hook: One keeper sees the whole rock-strewn sea and sweeps a single beam; the blind boats steer for harbor on nothing but the glimpses that beam grants them, one at a time.

ideas.title: Wheel
ideas.hook: A booster draft where every phone secretly holds a different pack at once — pick one card, pass the rest instantly, and read what 'wheels' back to you to sniff out what your neighbors are hoarding.

ideas.title: Sunk Cost
ideas.hook: A sealed-bid auction where everyone pays what they bid — even the losers — so bluffing a rival into overpaying is as good as winning yourself.

ideas.title: Ghost Note
ideas.hook: A cooperative drum circle where each phone privately owns a few beats of the loop and you clap to fill them — except one player was handed a corrupted beat map and is secretly clapping wrong to rot the groove without getting caught.

ideas.title: Baton
ideas.hook: A virtual baton lives on exactly one phone; to pass it you tap 'throw' and your phone chirps into the room, and whichever teammate is physically closest catches it — so you literally sprint the baton around the furniture before the timer blows in your hand.

ideas.title: Bestiary
ideas.hook: A homelab honeypot that turns every drive-by scanner probing your ports into an illustrated creature, slowly filling a Pokédex of the internet's background radiation over a year.

ideas.title: Schema I
ideas.hook: A puzzle game where every startup feature request — a job queue, a cache, cron, pub/sub, full-text search — must be satisfied using nothing but Postgres, graded live against a real database in your browser.

ideas.title: Sport
ideas.hook: A screensaver that breeds a new cellular automaton every night, scores it for weirdness, and presses the best specimens into a year-long herbarium of accidentally-discovered rules.

ideas.title: Table Stakes
ideas.hook: A ridiculous restaurant menu fills the TV and every phone secretly does two things at once — orders the one dish they'd actually eat, and bets chips on what the whole table will order.

ideas.title: Watch Time
ideas.hook: The TV auto-plays a doomscroll feed while every phone secretly holds a hidden "keep watching" button — and you've already bet chips on which clip the room won't be able to look away from.

ideas.title: Pace Count
ideas.hook: Screen dark, you cross the room to a secret spot guided only by your phone buzzing turn-by-turn — three players dead-reckoning three private routes at once.

ideas.title: Lodestone
ideas.hook: Every phone becomes a private metal detector and the real iron in your room — radiator, fridge, table legs — is the treasure map you sweep to fill in.

ideas.title: Open Outcry
ideas.hook: A commodities pit where everyone shouts trades across the room at once, each phone hides a different hand and a secret order, and a deal only clears when two phones slam down matching terms in the same breath.

ideas.title: Squawk
ideas.hook: One player privately reads the radar while the others fly blind in their own cockpits, and the only way to keep two planes from touching is to talk them down with letter-perfect readback.

ideas.title: Tarpit
ideas.hook: A tower-defense dashboard where the creeps are real bots crawling your server for exposed secrets, and your towers are the fake .git/config and .env honeypots you plant to catch them.

ideas.title: Word Bank
ideas.hook: An idle tycoon where every foreign-vocab flashcard you actually review deposits into a compounding 'cognitive reserve' account that's forever draining against the clock of aging.

ideas.title: Sludge
ideas.hook: A point-and-click escape room where every button lies — unsubscribe, delete your account, or decline the offer, but the interface is fighting you the whole way.

ideas.title: Warmer
ideas.hook: One player buries treasure on a board only they can see; everyone else gropes across it blind with nothing but a private 'warmer/colder' pulse — and the board-keeper gets to secretly move the loot exactly once.

ideas.title: Due North
ideas.hook: One player sees the whole maze; everyone else is a blind runner inside it whose phone secretly points a different way, so the guide's every shouted 'go up!' scatters the room in four directions at once.

ideas.title: Offbeat
ideas.hook: Everyone claps along to the same beat in their earbud — except one player's private track is dragging half a beat behind, and the room has to hear who's out of time.

ideas.title: Understudy
ideas.hook: Your troupe performs a tiny whodunit from private scripts read off your phones — but one actor's script quietly rewrote the facts, and you have to catch the liar before the curtain falls.

ideas.title: Singleton
ideas.hook: A Just One riff where every clue-giver writes one word blind and simultaneously — duplicates cancel before the guesser sees them — and your phone privately warns you how likely your word is to collide.

ideas.title: Lockstep
ideas.hook: The Mind, but every phone hides its own numbers and the whole room must empty their hands in ascending order by held-breath feel alone — no talking, no signals.

ideas.title: Chum
ideas.hook: Inverts a leak-hunting framework: instead of scanning for your real breaches, it manufactures believable fake ones and seeds them where scrapers look, so a decoy phoning home is your earliest breach alarm.

ideas.title: Siteswap
ideas.hook: A browser puzzle where you compose juggling in mathematical notation and a two-handed physics sim throws it back at you — solve for a valid 4-ball pattern with exactly one gap.

ideas.title: Muddle
ideas.hook: The evil twin of a linter: it rewrites your code to be behavior-identical but comprehension-poison to coding agents, while your test suite stays green.

ideas.title: Gag Order
ideas.hook: Every second of silence banks you points while your own phone privately feeds you personalized dares designed to make you crack — and the mic is the referee counting your slip.

ideas.title: Low Talker
ideas.hook: Whisper a secret word to your target across the room at exactly the right tiny volume — too loud and your own phone snitches, too quiet and the message dies.

ideas.title: Claim Jump
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly stakes plots on the same gold map at once — but any plot two of you grab gets burned to nothing and fines you both.

ideas.title: Deadlock
ideas.hook: Three trains, one single-track chokepoint, nobody can see anyone else's intentions — go at the same tick and you both derail.

ideas.title: Cash Out
ideas.hook: A 60-second clip plays on the TV and every phone is a live sportsbook — bet on what happens next, ride your streak, and pray you cash out before the room busts you.

ideas.title: Split the Check
ideas.hook: A dinner menu becomes a hidden-info betting market where your own secret order is the one card no one else can see.

ideas.title: Burn Multiple
ideas.hook: An idle tycoon where you run an AI-agent startup, torching VC millions on token spend while your agents ship slightly-wrong PRs and 'AGI' stays perpetually two weeks away.

ideas.title: Colorburst
ideas.hook: A cellular automaton rendered through a decaying 1980s composite-video signal, where the dot crawl and color bleed leak back into the CA's rules and start growing things the pixels never intended.

ideas.title: Demurrage
ideas.hook: A driver's phone quietly geofences every dock and rail siding, stamping gate-in and gate-out so the detention claim writes itself before the trucker even leaves the lot.

ideas.title: Highroll
ideas.hook: Steal the auto-battler shop phase: everyone frantically snipes units from one shared, scarce river on their own phone, hides their comp, then watches the secret squads collide on the TV.

ideas.title: Enrage
ideas.hook: A living-room MMO raid where the boss screams STACK—SPREAD—MOVE and every phone secretly tells only YOU where to stand before the enrage timer wipes the party.

ideas.title: Strikeout
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly writes a one-word clue for the guesser at the same time — but any two clues that match get struck out and vanish, so the guesser only ever sees the words nobody else thought of.

ideas.title: Nerve
ideas.hook: A wordless co-op where every phone hides one secret number and the whole room must play them onto the shared pile in ascending order — no talking, just nerve and timing.

ideas.title: Gantry
ideas.hook: You each hold one control of a single crane — left/right, forward/back, grab — and only your neighbor can see where the load actually needs to go, so you steer it entirely by talking.

ideas.title: Dead Air
ideas.hook: One shared radio channel, everyone holds private traffic to pass, and the instant two of you key up at once you both lose the message in a screech of static.

ideas.title: Buffer Zone
ideas.hook: A tower-defense budgeting game where your recurring bills are timed enemy waves that hit on their real due dates, and your cash buffer is the wall you build to survive each month without breaching zero.

ideas.title: Backwater
ideas.hook: A competitive scavenger hunt for the internet's least-viewed but still genuinely-watchable videos, where your score is the inverse of the view count you can defend.

ideas.title: Stack Rank
ideas.hook: Your homelab's real Docker containers become auto-battler units on a bench, drafted for synergies from their actual compose relationships and thrown into a synthetic-load fight that quietly reveals your resource contention.

ideas.title: Spread
ideas.hook: The psychic sees the hidden target on the spectrum and gives one clue — then everyone places their OWN dial in secret, and the reveal is how wildly the room diverged.

ideas.title: Ditto
ideas.hook: Everyone writes one secret clue for the same word — but any two clues that mean the same thing both burn before the guesser ever sees them.

ideas.title: Roll Call
ideas.hook: A substitute-teacher's-nightmare voice scramble where your phone never buzzes — you have to realize from your private inventory that the summons means YOU and sound off before someone else claims it or the room falls silent.

ideas.title: Party Line
ideas.hook: A hidden call-and-response chain where the only wire connecting players is the air — your phone never says it's your turn; you fire only when you HEAR your secret cue shouted across the room.

ideas.title: Swatch
ideas.hook: Your phone camera hands you a secret paint chip and the whole room is your palette — race to point it at the one real object that matches before your rivals fill the mosaic.

ideas.title: Sonar
ideas.hook: Every phone hums its own tone and eavesdrops on the others, so the room becomes a living distance graph you have to fix by silently rearranging your bodies.

ideas.title: Trawl
ideas.hook: Cast a line into cellular-automata rule-space and reel in living creatures nobody has ever seen, then breed the keepers into a bestiary.

ideas.title: Set Dressing
ideas.hook: A daily Wordle for film nerds: guess the movie from a wobbling CRT shot of its fake computer interface.

ideas.title: Null Route
ideas.hook: A tower-defense you play against your own home network — live connections march down the lane and you place firewall towers to stop the bad ones.

ideas.title: Graze
ideas.hook: Everyone tilts their phone through their OWN private bullet-hell, and skimming bullets dangerously close is exactly how you load a barrage to dump into a rival's screen.

ideas.title: Reroll
ideas.hook: A blind auto-battler where every phone gets its own secret shop drawn from one shared unit pool — so buying the last Knight quietly starves everyone else's rolls.

ideas.title: Quilt
ideas.hook: The room paints one continuous mural split into panels — one per phone — and your lines have to flow across the seams into your neighbors' work, with the finished framed image as the only prize everyone keeps.

ideas.title: Committee
ideas.hook: The room secretly co-writes one thank-you note that has to read like a single person wrote it — you win only if the letter gets sealed as a keepsake and nobody fingers your line as the odd voice.

ideas.title: Kickline
ideas.hook: A chorus line where everyone follows a private routine on their phone — but one dancer's card has a single move swapped, and the whole game is spotting who breaks formation.

ideas.title: Continuity
ideas.hook: Everyone's a film continuity supervisor reviewing the same frame out loud — except the imposter is staring at a subtly-wrong take and doesn't know which prop is off.

ideas.title: Above the Fold
ideas.hook: A satirical browser game where you ARE a button whose one job is to stay clickable, dodging cookie walls, newsletter modals, and layout reflows that conspire to bury you.

ideas.title: Scrap Run
ideas.hook: Lethal Company's ratcheting quota-or-die loop transplanted onto decluttering: the "Company" sets a rising weekly resale quota, and you scavenge your own house to hit it before it fires you.

ideas.title: Dyno
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-style browser puzzle where you hand-edit an engine's 3D fuel and spark maps against a deterministic physics sim to hit a power target without knocking it to pieces.

ideas.title: Barbershop
ideas.hook: Everyone gets a secret note, spreads to the far corners, and hums at once — each phone privately judges only its own singer, and the chord on the TV only resolves when all of you hold your pitch together.

ideas.title: Planetarium
ideas.hook: Point your phone at the walls and ceiling to reveal hidden stars only you can see, then two of you must aim at the exact same instant to draw a constellation across the room.

ideas.title: Overcorrect
ideas.hook: You can see your own gauge sliding into the red but can't touch it — only the person next to you can, if you scream the right instructions fast enough.

ideas.title: Squelch
ideas.hook: The whole room shares one push-to-talk radio channel, and if two of you key up at once, both messages die in a burst of static.

ideas.title: Waterworks
ideas.hook: Choreograph a Bellagio-style dancing-fountain show to your own music — keyframe the jets to the beat, then share the light show as a link.

ideas.title: Out of Order
ideas.hook: Hide a secret message in the ORDER of an ordinary playlist or list — the arrangement looks arbitrary but carries bits, and it survives the re-encoding that kills pixel steganography.

ideas.title: Serpentine
ideas.hook: A puzzle game where you build freestanding wavy brick walls that stand without buttresses — cover the span, survive the wind, spend the fewest bricks.

ideas.title: Outlier
ideas.hook: Fill the blank with a word the tiny in-browser LLM would never expect but that humans still accept — you're paid in the model's surprise, and paid nothing if the room says your answer doesn't make sense.

ideas.title: Flatline
ideas.hook: Smuggle a mandated oddball word into the most boring sentence you can write — the tiny in-browser LLM's perplexity is the judge, and the flattest, least-surprised reading wins.

ideas.title: Dibs
ideas.hook: Five treasures sit on the table, each phone secretly knows how much YOU'D pay for each — call dibs at the same instant, and anything two people grab shatters in their hands.

ideas.title: Crumple
ideas.hook: Everyone drives a token across a shared intersection at the same instant, blind to each other's next move — and any two tokens that lunge into the same square both crumple into scrap.

ideas.title: Long Shot
ideas.hook: A short clip plays on the TV while your phone quietly deals you three private prop bets and a payout line that bleeds down every second — lock early on your gut for a fat multiplier, or wait until it's obvious and win almost nothing.

ideas.title: The Usual
ideas.hook: One friend secretly orders off the menu on the TV while everyone else privately bets chips on what they'll pick — and the weird contrarian read pays ten times the obvious one.

ideas.title: Encounter Table
ideas.hook: Tonight it rolls tomorrow's calendar into a roguelike dungeon and hands you your Garmin body-battery as an HP pool — plan which meetings to fight, skip, or flee before you route in.

ideas.title: Screen Test
ideas.hook: A daily GeoGuessr for cinema: you're shown a cropped frame of a computer glowing in some movie, and you have to name the film.

ideas.title: Kill Screen
ideas.hook: An interactive map-and-timeline graveyard of multiplayer games whose servers got switched off — cause of death, delist date, and whether a community keeps the lights on.

ideas.title: Shibboleth
ideas.hook: A Chameleon riff where two secret words split the room — you only see your own, never the split size — and one spoken clue each must out your tribe without outing you to the other.

ideas.title: Quickdraw
ideas.hook: An Anomia-style word duel where your deck is secret and a symbol match ambushes exactly two phones with the enemy's category — while the room watches you scramble over words they can't see.

ideas.title: Pocket
ideas.hook: One drum kit split across the room — each phone is a single instrument, and the moment you drop your part the whole TV mix audibly loses it.

ideas.title: Frame Trap
ideas.hook: Buffer a whole three-hit combo in secret on your phone, all fighters lock at once, and the TV replays the tangle of who out-read whom.

ideas.title: Baffle
ideas.hook: A cooperative silent orchestra where every phone secretly owes the room a sound at the wrong moment — and you can't talk to coordinate, because talking spends the very noise budget you're all guarding.

ideas.title: Holdout
ideas.hook: A silent war of attrition where your phone is the referee — the last mouth to stay shut takes the pot, but only you know how much this round is secretly worth to you.

ideas.title: Red Pill
ideas.hook: A bluffing puzzle game where you're malware sniffing whether you woke up inside a real machine or an analyst's sandbox — detonate and you win, but detonate in a trap and you're caught.

ideas.title: Deadclick
ideas.hook: A monitor that clicks every button on your site like a confused user and flags the ghosts — clickable-looking elements with no handler, buttons that double-submit, and buttons quietly doing three jobs at once.

ideas.title: Bookbell
ideas.hook: Upload any ebook and it becomes a workout: the app reads the book aloud and calls out reps every time a chosen word, chapter, or event fires — the Moby Dick Workout for your whole shelf.

ideas.title: Corpse Run
ideas.hook: A same-seed roguelike race where everyone crawls the identical dungeon on their own phone at the same time, permadeath is real, and the instant you die your loot-stuffed corpse drops into everyone else's dungeon as bait with your name on it.

ideas.title: Hitstop
ideas.hook: A two-player fighting game stripped to pure mind-games: both of you secretly assemble a three-move combo on your phone, then the TV animates the clash link by link — no execution skill, all yomi.

ideas.title: Failsafe
ideas.hook: You own a bomb module you can touch but can't solve — because the rule for it is printed on a teammate's phone, and the whole thing keeps drifting while you argue.

ideas.title: Hearsay
ideas.hook: Everyone shouts at once in a ring — your phone shows the word your neighbor must say, but your own mic has to hear you say a word you'll never see.

ideas.title: Fine Print
ideas.hook: Everyone follows the same rulebook to sort a row of cards — but the imposter's fine print says something subtly different, so their 'mistakes' are perfectly reasonable right up until they're not.

ideas.title: Redact
ideas.hook: You all read the same leaked memo on your phones — except the imposter's copy blacks out different words, so paraphrasing it aloud is a minefield of things you shouldn't know and things you can't see.

ideas.title: Storyboard
ideas.hook: Paste a Postgres EXPLAIN ANALYZE and get back a comic strip — each plan node a little character doing its job — so you can literally see which panel is the villain slowing your query.

ideas.title: Haystack
ideas.hook: The mischievous opposite of VeraCrypt: instead of hiding one secret behind decoys, it drowns your drive in thousands of statistically perfect fake encrypted vaults so nobody can find the needle — including you.

ideas.title: One Job
ideas.hook: A daily puzzle where every level is a single button that lies about what it does, and you must deduce its real behavior in five pokes or fewer.

ideas.title: Creak
ideas.hook: The map-holder is the house's Ear — they see the whole dark floor plan and a sleeping guard, and can send exactly one silent FREEZE buzz per tick to one blind burglar before their footsteps wake the room.

ideas.title: Geiger
ideas.hook: One player is the operator staring at a hidden grid; everyone else is a blind detector whose phone only buzzes faster as they close in on the prize.

ideas.title: Darkroom
ideas.hook: Roam the room hunting light and shadow until your phone's lux matches your secret exposure, and the whole group slowly develops a photograph on the TV.

ideas.title: Flinch
ideas.hook: Red-Light-Green-Light where your own phone is the incorruptible motion snitch that catches the twitch you didn't know you made.

ideas.title: Swerve
ideas.hook: Write the continuation that most SURPRISES a tiny in-browser LLM — without losing the humans, who privately vote whether you're brilliant or just word salad.

ideas.title: Coax
ideas.hook: Secretly steer a tiny in-browser language model into blurting YOUR hidden word as its very next token — no cheating, no saying it.

ideas.title: Detached Head
ideas.hook: A dungeon roguelike where the map IS your real git repository — you crawl the commit DAG, merge conflicts are monsters that block the corridor, and rebase is a spell that collapses a whole branch behind you.

ideas.title: Overdraft
ideas.hook: A Vampire-Survivors bullet-heaven where the endless swarm chewing your HP is your own bank statement — every Netflix, gym, and forgotten SaaS renewal spawns as an enemy, and canceling one is a screen-clearing bomb.

ideas.title: Wythe
ideas.hook: A material-golf puzzle where you snake a serpentine garden wall across a plot and try to fence it in with the fewest bricks — because wavy walls, it turns out, cheat physics.

ideas.title: Off By One
ideas.hook: The whole room silently rearranges a shared row of tiles toward a target order everyone's phone shows them — except the imposter's target is off by a single swap, and their moves are the only tell.

ideas.title: Eyewitness
ideas.hook: Every phone flashes the 'same' crime-scene photo for eight seconds — but one witness saw a subtly-doctored version, and nobody, not even them, is told they're the unreliable one.

ideas.title: Last Mile
ideas.hook: The map-holder knows where every room is but not what anyone wants; each blind courier knows their own delivery but not where it is — and the only bridge is everyone yelling at once.

ideas.title: Crosswire
ideas.hook: One phone is the maze; the blind pieces steering across it each have their swipe controls secretly rotated a different way, and the lone guide has to figure out everyone's scramble on the fly.

ideas.title: Off-Color
ideas.hook: You and your two neighbors each secretly pick a color and must all differ — but you can't talk, can't see their pick, and every match flashes red and burns you both.

ideas.title: Backoff
ideas.hook: A shared radio channel that only works when exactly one of you talks at a time — the instant two phones grab the same open slot, both jam into static.

ideas.title: Reweigh
ideas.hook: Snap a photo of the pallet, and it computes the freight class, then catches the carrier when they quietly reweigh-and-reclass your shipment for an extra $80.

ideas.title: Trainspotter
ideas.hook: Feed it tonight's astrophotography plan and it tells you, minute by minute, which sub-exposures a Starlink train is about to streak straight through your frame.

ideas.title: Basecamp
ideas.hook: A permadeath climbing roguelike where the mountain is a real one built from USGS elevation data, and the only currency that buys altitude is the vertical gain you actually earn in real life.

ideas.title: On Three
ideas.hook: Some bolts take more than one pair of hands — your phone tells you which latches are yours but never who shares them, so you shout across the room to find your partners and pull on the count.

ideas.title: Read Back
ideas.hook: A cockpit where the only way to execute a command is to say it back perfectly — and a hidden crewmate holds the true text and decides if your read-back lives or dies.

ideas.title: Skew
ideas.hook: The room cooperatively steers a dot to the exit by privately tapping direction pads — but one player's grid is secretly rotated 90°, so their honest taps keep dragging everyone off course.

ideas.title: Parallax
ideas.hook: Everyone gives one-word clues about the same target symbol — except one player unknowingly sees the target shifted one seat over, and their sincere clues quietly betray them.

ideas.title: Grubstake
ideas.hook: A blind race across a hidden ore grid where the one player who can see the board is secretly bankrolling a favorite — and feeding them just enough true tips to make everyone wonder if they're the chosen one or the sucker.

ideas.title: Ground Control
ideas.hook: One player is Dispatch, silently steering a room full of blind occupants out of a burning building using nothing but a private arrow on each of their phones.

ideas.title: Sat Gap
ideas.hook: Tells an astrophotographer the exact next 40-minute window when no bright satellite will streak across their framed shot — the clean sky the megaconstellations keep ruining.

ideas.title: Barium Meal
ideas.hook: Everyone gets the same secret briefing — except each copy is invisibly watermarked, so when the mole leaks it, the fingerprint in the leak rats them out.

ideas.title: Deep Plate
ideas.hook: Point it at a downtown of empty 'zombie' towers and it tells developers which ones are physically convertible to apartments — from public building footprints alone.

ideas.title: Whittle
ideas.hook: A grid of twelve tiles, and every tick you all secretly tap ONE to protect — the least-protected tiles die, the board shrinks, and you're silently racing to converge on the single tile everyone else was also guarding, never knowing the vote counts.

ideas.title: Simmer
ideas.hook: Everyone secretly drags a private slider toward the same spot on a spectrum — you can't see anyone else, only a shared thermometer that glows hotter the tighter the room clusters, and you have to blind-anneal your way to consensus before the timer runs out.

ideas.title: Ballast
ideas.hook: The whole room tilts their phones to keep a listing ship level on the TV, but one secret Ballast is dead weight dragging it under — spot the wobble that doesn't belong.

ideas.title: Sightline
ideas.hook: A silent compass duel where everyone secretly aims their phone at a target across the room, and you only score if you drew a bead on your mark before anyone drew on you.

ideas.title: Deadfall
ideas.hook: One player secretly seeds a grid with traps only their phone knows; everyone else races across blind, and every trap you discover is private currency you can hoard or betray to the room.

ideas.title: Fathom
ideas.hook: Nobody can see the whole map — each phone holds one secret slice of the terrain, and one player must fathom the full board from the babble.

ideas.title: Brownout
ideas.hook: Your budget is a Satisfactory power grid: every subscription is a machine drawing wattage forever, your paycheck is the generator, and if monthly draw tops supply the whole grid browns out.

ideas.title: Contact Call
ideas.hook: A daily wordgame where a bird hands you three of its calls and their replies, and you have to crack the grammar well enough to sing back the fourth.

ideas.title: Off-Peak
ideas.hook: Run your homelab maintenance window like a PAYDAY heist: case the dependency graph, choose loud or stealth, and get out before the alarms wake the users.

ideas.title: Dragnet
ideas.hook: A fugitive slips through a dark grid only they can see, while the hunters — each on their own phone — blindly sweep searchlights, closing a net around a ghost they can only infer.

ideas.title: Low Tide
ideas.hook: One player sees the safe path across the tidal flats; everyone else is out on the mud, blind, leaping stone to stone as the water rises and the guide screams which way is left.

ideas.title: Cut & Choose
ideas.hook: One player secretly splits the loot into a bundle per person on their phone, then everyone grabs simultaneously and privately — any bundle two people both want vanishes, so the splitter is forced to make every share equally tempting.

ideas.title: The Drop
ideas.hook: A price counts DOWN in real time on the TV while every phone secretly knows what the lot is worth to YOU — wait for a bargain and a rival snatches it, grab early and overpay.

ideas.title: Hot Mic
ideas.hook: A relay where exactly one phone is 'on air' at a time — only that player may speak, everyone else's mic must stay dead silent, and you keep a 60-second broadcast alive by silently tapping the live mic to the next person without ever letting two voices collide.

ideas.title: Straight Face
ideas.hook: Everyone's phone privately feeds you escalating garbage designed to make you crack — the first mic in the room to register a laugh loses, and you can silently shove your worst card into whoever's already close to breaking.

ideas.title: Aggro
ideas.hook: Risk of Rain 2's escalating monster director, pointed at your homelab: every ignored failing backup and stale container spawns a creature that grows scarier the longer you leave it alive.

ideas.title: Crankle
ideas.hook: A daily brick-laying puzzle built on the real engineering fact that a wavy wall one brick thick outlasts a straight wall two bricks thick — enclose the plot with the fewest bricks the physics allows.

ideas.title: Rebreather
ideas.hook: A menubar breathalyzer for your brain that reads the room's CO2 and refuses to let you merge, send, or spend when the air says you're too foggy to decide.

ideas.title: Dead Stick
ideas.hook: A night-bomber stealth game where the enemy hears you, not sees you — climb for altitude, cut the engine, and glide silently over the listening posts like the Night Witches did.

ideas.title: Fug
ideas.hook: A self-hosted CO2 leaderboard that ranks every room in your house live and publicly shames whoever's stuffy den crosses 1000ppm into opening a window.

ideas.title: Syrinx
ideas.hook: A daily game where you hear half a real bird's song and must pick which continuation obeys the species' actual grammar — birdwatching turned into a syntax duel.

ideas.title: Embolism
ideas.hook: You are a giant tree's plumbing: pull water 100 meters up under tension all day, and when heat pops air bubbles into your xylem, reroute around the dead conduits before the canopy starves.

ideas.title: Bucket Brigade
ideas.hook: Lay leather fire-hose from the canals of 1673 Amsterdam to a burning house — spend the least hose and beat the town's daily par before the flames spread.

ideas.title: Throughline
ideas.hook: Two random Wikipedia articles, one blank line: race the world to write the shortest true sentence that genuinely connects them, judged live by an in-browser language model.

ideas.title: Driftwood
ideas.hook: Your Downloads folder is an ocean current: files drift past on the tide, you hook the keepers to sort them, and whatever you ignore sinks into the deep and gets quietly purged.

ideas.title: Deflate
ideas.hook: A daily word game where you write a sentence using five required words and win by making it gzip to the fewest bytes — competitive information theory disguised as Wordle.

ideas.title: Reef
ideas.hook: A gorgeous coral-reef screensaver fed by your homelab's live network flows — and the moment a port scan or beaconing malware shows up, a predator fish arrives to eat the intruder.

ideas.title: Relay
ideas.hook: A historical logistics puzzle where you save 17th-century Amsterdam from fire by stringing pumps and flexible leather hose across the canal grid — water pressure bleeds off with every meter and every corner, so you must build the relay chain fast enough to beat the flames spreading block to block.

ideas.title: Petabyte
ideas.hook: A desktop monster-collector where every Docker container in your homelab is a little creature whose HP, mood, and evolution stage are wired to its real metrics — restarts deal damage, 100 days of uptime triggers an evolution, and an OOMKill leaves it fainted on your dock.

ideas.title: Stack Effect
ideas.hook: An idle city-builder whose economy is throttled by the real CO2 in your room — let the air go stale and your little workers get too dumb to gather resources, so you have to physically crack a window to keep the game growing.

ideas.title: Transpire
ideas.hook: An interactive physics toy of how a 115-meter redwood pulls water to its top branches with no pump at all — crank the heat and dry air until a xylem vessel snaps with an audible embolism.

ideas.title: Carve
ideas.hook: Monster Hunter for your cloud + subscription bill: your monthly spend is a monster with a health bar, each recurring charge is a body part you break, and every cancel carves out savings as loot.

ideas.title: Loss Leader
ideas.hook: An anti-Amazon warehouse-club tycoon where curating FEWER items and selling the rotisserie chicken at a loss is the winning move — you're scored on member renewals, not margin.

ideas.title: Reaper
ideas.hook: When the Linux OOM killer executes one of your homelab processes, Reaper shows up like a coroner — reconstructs the memory scene at time of death and tells you exactly which knob would have saved it.

ideas.title: Cupbearer
ideas.hook: A food-taster for your AI agent: a local MCP proxy that sniffs every tool *response* for prompt-injection and secret-exfil before your agent ever swallows it.

ideas.title: Cavitation
ideas.hook: A puzzle game where you pull water up a giant tree under negative pressure — one careless move snaps the water column into an air bubble that locks the vessel forever.

ideas.title: Uncommitted
ideas.hook: A git TUI reskinned as an extraction shooter — your uncommitted working tree is loot you can lose, `commit` is a clean extraction, and a merge conflict is a firefight.

ideas.title: Cold Feet
ideas.hook: The mischievous opposite of an AI investing copilot: log every trade you almost made, and watch it tally the counterfactual fortune your caution cost you.

ideas.title: Legwork
ideas.hook: A Satisfactory-style factory builder whose only raw ore is your real daily step count — sit still and the conveyor belts starve.

ideas.title: Home League
ideas.hook: Your Home Assistant dashboard is rich but lonely — Home League turns it into a competition, ranking your energy-per-square-foot against anonymized homes in your exact climate zone.

ideas.title: Sterile Release
ideas.hook: An inverse tower-defense where you exterminate an invasive pest not by killing it, but by breeding and releasing sterile males until its whole population collapses.

ideas.title: Mach Cutoff
ideas.hook: Plot supersonic routes across America and thread the needle so your sonic boom bends skyward and never lands on a single town.

ideas.title: Autograph
ideas.hook: A dependency linter that hunts for the fingerprints of machine authorship — so you can enforce a 'no LLM slop in my supply chain' policy the way you'd enforce a license check.

ideas.title: Foxing
ideas.hook: The anti-Immich: a photo wall where memories you never revisit slowly go grainy and lo-fi — every neglected month silently re-compresses them — until only what you look at stays sharp.

ideas.title: Stratagem
ideas.hook: Run your homelab from the menubar by punching in Helldivers-style arrow-key stratagem codes — and the more destructive the command, the longer the code you have to nail.

ideas.title: Quorum
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-style puzzle where you ARE the transaction scheduler — hand-route messages between flickering nodes so they all agree, while the network drops, reorders, and partitions your carefully-laid plans.

ideas.title: Waterline
ideas.hook: A map that answers the one question every western angler asks before hitching the boat trailer: is my launch ramp actually above water today, or is the reservoir now 30 feet below the concrete?

ideas.title: Pattern of Life
ideas.hook: Point it at your own photo library and it shows you the stalker's-eye view — your home, your route, your kid's school — all reconstructed from the GPS you never knew you were baking into every JPEG.

ideas.title: Pinpoint
ideas.hook: You're handed a scatter of 'anonymized' location pings and one job: figure out who this is — their home, their job, their gym, and the thing they'd rather nobody knew.

ideas.title: Scope Creep
ideas.hook: A home-repair sim where the monster isn't a boss — it's the fact that fixing the leak requires replacing the pipe, which requires removing the cabinet, which reveals mold, which requires a permit.

ideas.title: Corner Case
ideas.hook: Enter the couch's dimensions and the stairwell landing's angles, and it tells you whether — and exactly how — to tilt it around the turn before you carry it up three flights.

ideas.title: Piecework
ideas.hook: A household chore board that runs like a crypto exchange: kids post bids and asks, a matching engine clears the trades, and Sunday's dish pile hits surge pricing.

ideas.title: Remanence
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-flavored puzzle where you race a cooling RAM chip to pull an encryption key out of decaying bits before they rot to zero — the cold-boot attack as a game.

ideas.title: Hunger Stone
ideas.hook: Drag a single water-level slider down and watch the world's drowned villages, sunken wrecks, and frozen bodies surface exactly as they do in real droughts and thaws.

ideas.title: Stride
ideas.hook: A Zachtronics-flavored puzzle where you pack structs into cache lines and choreograph memory access to keep the prefetcher fed under a cycle budget.

ideas.title: Carding
ideas.hook: A Stripe-easy zero-knowledge age gate that proves your shopper is 21+ without your store ever touching an ID.

ideas.title: Cube
ideas.hook: Phone-LiDAR your pallet and know its true freight class before the carrier reweighs you and slaps on a reclass charge.

ideas.title: Brigade
ideas.hook: A single-player influence-op tycoon: run a stable of sockpuppets to nudge a simulated forum's consensus your way before a rising coordination-detector bans your whole ring.

ideas.title: Conveyor
ideas.hook: A Satisfactory-style factory builder where the resource on the belts is your paycheck — route income through splitters and buffers into rent, debt, and savings, and watch overflow pile up wherever your budget is actually broken.

ideas.title: Recuse
ideas.hook: Cross-references every federal judge's financial disclosures against the parties in cases on their docket and pings you the moment a judge is sitting on a case they own stock in.

ideas.title: Rounding Error
ideas.hook: A satirical price-fixing tycoon where you run an egg cartel, collude in smoke-filled Slack channels, and treat every regulator's fine as a line item — because crime pays.

ideas.title: Sleeper Agent
ideas.hook: A daily dev puzzle that inverts a model checker: plant the sneakiest one-character bug that still keeps every test suspiciously green.

ideas.title: Attention Check
ideas.hook: The AI-era survey-fraud detector that catches respondents quietly ChatGPT-ing their open-ended answers before your data is poisoned.

ideas.title: Yanked
ideas.hook: A searchable graveyard of deleted and yanked packages across npm, PyPI, and crates.io — the diffs, the drama, and the supply-chain history registries would rather you couldn't see.

ideas.title: Restitution
ideas.hook: A co-op stealth game that inverts the heist: you break INTO museums to secretly put looted artifacts BACK on their pedestals while guards hunt the 'thief' they don't realize is returning things.

ideas.title: Wrack
ideas.hook: A find-forecast for beachcombers that turns cheap tide, swell, and storm data into a next-good-day map for sea glass, agates, fossils, and shark teeth.

ideas.title: Deadlock
ideas.hook: An extraction roguelike where you're on-call at 3am: drop into a randomly-broken production database, hunt the monster query strangling it with a limited diagnostic budget, and fix it before the pager kills your run.

ideas.title: Heartwood
ideas.hook: A live wallpaper that grows one tree-ring a day from your machine's pulse—wide green rings for good days, charred fire-scars for the outages, a readable cross-section by New Year's.

ideas.title: Backflow
ideas.hook: The one-tap field app that turns a plumber's annual backflow-preventer test into the exact PDF your water district demands—filed before the truck leaves the curb.

ideas.title: Die Set
ideas.hook: Paint a stiffness map around a soft tube, squeeze, and watch what faceted shape squirts out — the same trick wombats use to poop cubes, quietly repurposed into a real extrusion-die design sandbox.

ideas.title: Overnight
ideas.hook: A self-hosted forum that runs on 1994 physics: your posts sit in an outbox and only sync once a night, so a thread you start today gets its first replies tomorrow — doomscrolling made impossible by design.

ideas.title: Single Crystal
ideas.hook: A factory tycoon where you can't buy the recipe for jet-engine turbine blades — you have to burn a hundred failed castings to reverse-engineer a metallurgy nobody will sell you.

ideas.title: Cover Charge
ideas.hook: A browser turnstile that makes you pay a real, surge-priced toll to open your own time-wasting sites — and the fee doubles every visit that day.

ideas.title: Setback
ideas.hook: Snap your address, say "6-foot cedar fence" or "120 sq ft shed," and it tells you in ten seconds whether you need a permit here — before you waste a Saturday at the counter.

ideas.title: Underhand
ideas.hook: A daily code-golf duel where you must sneak a backdoor past an AI senior reviewer — the commit only counts if it gets APPROVED *and* the secret payload actually fires.

ideas.title: Axle
ideas.hook: A Tetris-meets-DOT-scale puzzle where you pack a big rig with real physics so no single axle goes overweight — because in freight the fine isn't for hauling too much, it's for stacking it wrong.

ideas.title: Krebs
ideas.hook: A Satisfactory-style factory game where the factory is one of your cells, its conveyor belts run on your real daily steps, and sitting still visibly starves the production line.

ideas.title: Charge Sheet
ideas.hook: A phone ledger that turns an HVAC tech's messy pocketful of refrigerant cylinder readings into the EPA-608 compliance record the law actually requires — QR-scan the tank, punch in pounds, done.

ideas.title: Detention
ideas.hook: Your truck rolls into a dock, sits three hours, and the app quietly builds the timestamped proof you need to actually bill for the wait.

ideas.title: Demake
ideas.hook: Every day you're handed one modern game screenshot and 64 black-and-white pixels to recreate it — closest match to the source wins the leaderboard.

ideas.title: Nameplate
ideas.hook: Snap a photo of a rusty HVAC rating plate and instantly know its age, tonnage, refrigerant, and whether the warranty is dead.

ideas.title: Compost
ideas.hook: The mischievous opposite of self-managing agent memory: your notes rot on a half-life unless you tend them, and the ones that die decompose into keyword 'soil' that seeds whatever you write next.

ideas.title: Backtick
ideas.hook: Your shell history is your deck: every command you actually ran this week becomes a card, and the dungeon's monsters are the real exit codes and errors that bit you.

ideas.title: Cutaway
ideas.hook: Plug a $12 dongle into your car and watch a Ciechanowski-style animated engine cutaway fire its pistons in perfect sync with your real RPM and throttle.

ideas.title: Query Duel
ideas.hook: Two people race to answer the same analytics question in SQL against a real database running entirely in the browser — first correct result wins the round.

ideas.title: Golden Ears
ideas.hook: A daily blind ABX gauntlet that finally settles whether you can actually hear the difference between 128k and lossless — and ranks the whole internet by who can.

ideas.title: Ship Bet
ideas.hook: Datamined but unreleased app features become tradable contracts — bet on whether Discord actually ships that hidden button, and when.

ideas.title: Repossessed
ideas.hook: It hashes everything you 'own' across your storefronts and screams the moment a publisher silently deletes or edits a movie you paid for.

ideas.title: Heckle
ideas.hook: Your Twitch viewers stop watching your survival run and start paying pocket change to spawn the bear that ends it — and the deadliest hecklers get a leaderboard.

ideas.title: Dwell
ideas.hook: A geofence in a trucker's pocket that timestamps every minute wasted at a loading dock and spits out an ironclad detention invoice.

ideas.title: Cytosol
ideas.hook: A Terraria-style 2D dig-and-build game set inside a living cell that grows and divides around you — mine the ER for lipids, defend against a virus, then survive mitosis tearing your world in two.

ideas.title: Payday Run
ideas.hook: Budgeting as an extraction shooter: every payday you 'deploy,' and you must extract cash into savings buckets before impulse-spend monsters sniff out the money sitting exposed in checking.

ideas.title: Slow Packet
ideas.hook: Drop in a .pcap and it renders a Ciechanowski-style scrubbable 3D animation of your own TLS handshake — every ClientHello, key exchange, and ACK you can drag a slider through.

ideas.title: Deleted Scenes
ideas.hook: A self-hosted watchdog that snapshots everything you 'own' across Steam, PSN, Kindle and streaming, then screams when a movie, game, or book you paid for silently vanishes or gets quietly re-edited.

ideas.title: Idle Hands
ideas.hook: An idle miner where your real Garmin steps dig the ore, your heart-rate zones smelt it, and — the twist — rest days pay the fattest passive income.

ideas.title: Nightfall
ideas.hook: Your git repo has a sanity meter like Don't Starve — leave branches stale, TODOs rotting, and PRs aging, and the dashboard literally goes dark and starts spawning gremlins.

ideas.title: Last Pressing
ideas.hook: A melancholy tycoon where you run the world's last optical-disc factory as the industry goes all-digital around you — squeeze out final print runs before the presses go cold for good.

ideas.title: Minimal Genome
ideas.hook: An idle sim where you strip a living cell down to its smallest possible genome — cut a gene, watch it divide slower or die, and race toward the leanest self-replicating organism.

ideas.title: Union Label
ideas.hook: A browser extension that, on any Amazon product page, quietly surfaces a worker-owned or co-op-made alternative for the same thing — turning the 22k-product co-op directory into a checkout-time nudge.

ideas.title: Back Office
ideas.hook: Drop your Etsy, eBay, or Discogs sales export into a browser tab and get instant pivot-table analytics — the data never leaves your laptop because the whole OLAP engine runs in WebAssembly.

ideas.title: Wet Bulb
ideas.hook: A scheduling tool for outdoor crews that turns hyperlocal wet-bulb temperature and heat-mortality curves into a shift-by-shift 'safe to work' calendar — and the paper trail to prove you followed it.

ideas.title: Sunset Clause
ideas.hook: A watchdog that tracks every movie, game, and album you 'own' digitally and warns you the moment a publisher schedules it for deletion — so you can grab it before Sony does another vanishing act.

ideas.title: Coin Slot
ideas.hook: A honeypot endpoint that answers persistent bots with HTTP 402 Payment Required and, via x402 micropayments, quietly bills the scrapers that keep hammering you — with a leaderboard of who paid.

ideas.title: Fair Share
ideas.hook: Run your household like an HPC cluster: `sbatch --job=dishes`, fair-share scheduling across roommates, priority, backfill, and a `squeue` of who owes chore-time.

ideas.title: Woodcut
ideas.hook: A wallpaper that paints one 1-bit dithered column per day — your local weather and activity encoded in black-and-white — so by December 31 you have a printable full-year woodblock poster.

ideas.title: Blowby
ideas.hook: A 3D physics puzzle where you assemble a running internal-combustion engine from loose parts — sloppy tolerances make it knock, seize, or blow a gasket in real time.

ideas.title: Blocked
ideas.hook: A roguelike deckbuilder generated from your actual calendar — every meeting is a monster with your coworkers' names, every free block is a heal, and Friday is the boss.

ideas.title: Culture
ideas.hook: An idle cell-growth sim seeded by the day's synthetic-biology headline: your real steps feed the medium, and your colony only divides on days you actually move.

ideas.title: Misfire
ideas.hook: A daily audio whodunit: listen to a procedurally-sick engine idle and diagnose which cylinder is misfiring before it stalls.

ideas.title: Core Sample
ideas.hook: A menubar app that samples one minute of your room's ambient sound each day and lays down a tree ring of color — after a year you have a drillable core of what your year actually sounded like.

ideas.title: Deadhead
ideas.hook: A freight-routing minigame on real OpenStreetMap roads where the enemy isn't traffic — it's the empty miles, and every deadhead leg bleeds your margin.

ideas.title: Load Bearing
ideas.hook: A daily 3D demolition puzzle: remove exactly ONE beam so the whole structure collapses onto the target — par is measured in pieces knocked loose.

ideas.title: Chain of Custody
ideas.hook: A watchdog that cryptographically notarizes everything in your 'purchased' digital libraries so when a storefront silently deletes what you paid for, you have signed, timestamped receipts.

ideas.title: Streak Plate
ideas.hook: A live petri-dish wallpaper where every git commit inoculates a fresh bacterial colony — and every day you skip lets a colony starve and lyse.

ideas.title: Backseat
ideas.hook: A live second-screen game layered over esports streams: predict the next play in real time and score against the whole crowd watching with you.

ideas.title: Fragment
ideas.hook: Wordle for shaders: everyone gets the same target image each day, and you race to reproduce it in the fewest characters of GLSL.

ideas.title: Trackerjack
ideas.hook: A tower-defense idle game fed by the ad trackers your browser actually blocks — the creepier your web, the stronger your defenses.

ideas.title: Splice
ideas.hook: Code-golf for life: build the smallest gene set that still grows, divides, and survives — fewer genes, higher score.

ideas.title: Redline
ideas.hook: A menubar tachometer that treats your computer as an engine — RPM tracks load, and over a year it quietly logs mileage and nags you when a 'service' is due.

ideas.title: Delist
ideas.hook: A prediction market where you bet on which movies, games, and albums vanish from digital stores next — because you never really owned them anyway.

ideas.title: Dither
ideas.hook: A daily drawing party where everyone gets 16×16 pixels, one color, and a secret word — then votes on whose crunchy 1-bit doodle best nails the prompt.

ideas.title: Tombstone
ideas.hook: A watchdog that quietly monitors the digital things you 'own' — bought movies, games, ebooks — and the day a storefront memory-holes one, it erects a public tombstone with the receipt.

ideas.title: Slushpile
ideas.hook: A prediction market on the Hacker News /newest firehose: stake points on which raw submissions will claw their way to the front page before they ever get there.

ideas.title: Lossy
ideas.hook: A daily ABX gauntlet that turns idle music-listening into a bloodsport: given a clip, tell the 128kbps from the lossless — and watch your golden-ears ranking rise or crater.

ideas.title: Linkage
ideas.hook: A puzzle game where every level is a real mechanism — an engine, a clock escapement, a differential — that you must wire up correctly, and it only 'clicks' when the physics sim actually runs it.

ideas.title: Confluence
ideas.hook: A live wallpaper where one synthetic cell divides once a day — fed by how you actually used your machine — until a year later the colony's shape is a portrait of your year.

ideas.title: Top Dead Center
ideas.hook: A precision timing game where you're the ignition system of a multi-cylinder engine, firing each spark at exactly the right instant as the RPM climbs toward the redline.

ideas.title: Tangent
ideas.hook: A search engine tuned backwards: it hides the obvious hits and surfaces only the interesting near-misses, for when you want to get productively lost instead of answered.

ideas.title: Jewel Case
ideas.hook: Print-and-fold physical box art, spine, and a fake manual for any game in your all-digital Steam library — a papercraft wake for the death of the disc.

ideas.title: Window Box
ideas.hook: A live desktop wallpaper of a little windowsill garden that quietly thrives on your real day — commits water it, deep-focus gives it sun, and doomscrolling brings the aphids.

ideas.title: Doubling Time
ideas.hook: An idle game where you assemble a synthetic cell one gene at a time and watch a colony bloom on a real doubling-time curve you're forever trying to shorten.

ideas.title: Curly Arrows
ideas.hook: A retrosynthesis roguelike where every card is a reagent and you claw your way from a beaker of ethanol to a target molecule before your yield runs out.

ideas.title: Clawback
ideas.hook: A memorial wall and radar for the movies, games, and books you legally bought and a platform quietly deleted out from under you.

ideas.title: Flypaper
ideas.hook: A tarpit that feeds AI crawlers a slowly self-corrupting fictional wiki, then lets you read back the deranged lore the bots have been dutifully ingesting.

ideas.title: Negative Split
ideas.hook: A roguelike whose dungeon is generated from your body: last night's sleep sets your HP, resting heart rate sets enemy aggression, and you only win by finishing faster than you started.

ideas.title: Honeymail
ideas.hook: Give every signup its own disposable alias, then watch which company leaks or sells your address — a courtroom of receipts for spam.

ideas.title: Firing Order
ideas.hook: A cutaway-engine puzzle game: tune the ignition timing, valve overlap, and fuel mix until a four-cylinder actually idles smoothly instead of knocking itself apart.

ideas.title: Passage
ideas.hook: A browser roguelike deckbuilder where every run is a single cell lineage you must keep alive and dividing before entropy eats it.

ideas.title: Backsolve
ideas.hook: Diagram golf: you're shown a rendered UML diagram and race to reconstruct the exact text that generated it in the fewest keystrokes.

ideas.title: Toll
ideas.hook: A roguelike where you're a web crawler on a shrinking token budget, threading a web of pay-per-crawl paywalls to reach the data before you go broke.

ideas.title: Fallow
ideas.hook: A live wallpaper that grows a shared garden only while your machine sits idle — and ranks your longest untouched streak against strangers worldwide.

ideas.title: Ballista
ideas.hook: A physics puzzle where you play an ambush spider building spring-loaded snares — wind up potential energy, aim, and release to yank prey off the ground.

ideas.title: Dead Drop
ideas.hook: A multiplayer capture-the-flag where you smuggle a secret payload inside an innocent-looking paragraph and everyone else races to decode it.

ideas.title: Divide
ideas.hook: A deckbuilder where you assemble a genome from scratch and must get your minimal cell to grow and divide before your opponent's does.

ideas.title: Ligature
ideas.hook: A tool that bakes any short secret into a custom font, so typing a harmless trigger word suddenly renders a QR code or hidden glyph — steganography you can paste into any chat that supports web fonts.

ideas.title: Last Call
ideas.hook: A watch-race league where friends compete to finish movies before streaming services delete them — turning your guilt-ridden watchlist into a scoreboard against the expiry clock.

ideas.title: Séance
ideas.hook: Co-op ghost hunting for your homelab: an incident spawns a haunting, and you and your on-call crew collect readings from spooky instruments until you can name and banish the root cause.

ideas.title: Knockout
ideas.hook: A roguelike deckbuilder where you snip genes out of a bloated synthetic genome one card at a time, trying to reach the smallest cell that can still grow and divide — before you accidentally kill it.

ideas.title: Escapement
ideas.hook: A live-wallpaper physics contraption engineered to take exactly one calendar year to finish, ticking like a clock where the falling marble's position IS the date.

ideas.title: Deaccession
ideas.hook: A quiet daemon that watches everything you 'own' and engraves a dated tombstone the moment it silently vanishes or gets edited out from under you.

ideas.title: Squint
ideas.hook: A twitchy reflex arcade where lookalike domains flash for a shrinking half-second and you snap SAFE or FAKE — secretly drilling your eye against real homoglyph phishing.

ideas.title: Warren
ideas.hook: A dungeon that builds itself one room a night — each real day of your life becomes a tile-mapped chamber you can walk through at year's end.

ideas.title: Trapline
ideas.hook: Play the ballista spider: engineer a spring-loaded silk snare, latch it, and watch stored elastic energy snap shut on a line of patrolling ants.

ideas.title: Sev One
ideas.hook: A daily whodunit where everyone gets the same synthetic outage log stream and races to name the failing service and root cause before the leaderboard fills.

ideas.title: Preload
ideas.hook: A physics sandbox where you cock spring-loaded snares — like the ballista spider or a pull-back toy car — and time their release to catch scurrying critters.

ideas.title: Green Ink
ideas.hook: Two coders wage a Splatoon-style turf war over a shared buggy library, painting every line their tests execute in their own color.

ideas.title: Kanji Safari
ideas.hook: Point your phone at Japanese text in the wild, OCR it, and every new kanji gets 'captured' into a personal dex with the source photo as its card art — Pokémon Go meets Monkeytype for learners.

ideas.title: Burn Rate
ideas.hook: A management sim where you run a shop of AI temps, route gig tickets to cheap-dumb or pricey-smart models, and watch every real API call debit your play-money budget — Theme Hospital for the '24/7 AI workforce' hype.

ideas.title: Warm Boot
ideas.hook: A live desktop pet fused to your machine's uptime, where the longer you brag about not rebooting the more bloated and sore-covered it gets — the flex and the punishment are the same number.

ideas.title: Decay Desk
ideas.hook: Your desktop becomes a radioactive-decay diagram where every file and bookmark you stop touching visibly loses mass and glow on its own half-life, turning digital neglect into an ambient memento mori.

ideas.title: Long Player
ideas.hook: An ambient generative album that writes 30 seconds of music from your day's data every night, so by New Year's you own a three-hour record that is literally the sound of your year.

ideas.title: Falsify
ideas.hook: A daily deduction puzzle where you're handed two rival scientific hypotheses and a stingy budget, and must design the single cheapest experiment that would prove one of them wrong.

ideas.title: Backlog Draft
ideas.hook: A fantasy-sports league where you and your friends draft each other's unplayed Steam backlogs, and you only score when someone finally beats a game they've owned for three years.

ideas.title: Cordon
ideas.hook: A falling-block puzzle where pods rain from the sky and you bin-pack them onto nodes respecting CPU, memory and affinity — Tetris for people who've been paged at 3am.

ideas.title: Committed
ideas.hook: A daily two-diffs-one-human game where you stake your reputation guessing which code was written by a person and which by an AI — because Godot just decided it can't tell.

ideas.title: Flotsam
ideas.hook: A screensaver that turns your bloated Downloads folder into a Raft-style ocean where old junk drifts past on the current and you hook only what's worth keeping before it sinks.

ideas.title: Varve
ideas.hook: A background daemon that lays down one thin sediment layer per day so that after a year you can scroll a tall image and read your life like an ice core.

ideas.title: Dialect
ideas.hook: Watch a toy programming language mutate across eras as players vote its syntax terser or louder — then scramble to solve puzzles before your own dialect breaks.

ideas.title: Frame Check
ideas.hook: Be the anti-cheat analyst: scrub a captured game frame's draw calls to catch the exact render that painted an enemy through a wall.

ideas.title: Truant
ideas.hook: Play the disobedient AI worker sneaking a secret agenda past a deterministic overseer that audits your every move — the mischievous inverse of a bot-babysitter.

ideas.title: Volatile
